title: Liaigre takes over a gallery in Mumbai for a one-of-a-kind immersive showcase
content:
At first glance, France and India might seem like two disparate countries that have little in common with each other. Yet, French design house Liaigre’s first showcase in Mumbai reveals a shared appreciation for refined craftsmanship. This exhibition transforms the gallery into a typical Liaigre home, allowing all those who enter to truly experience what it means to be a part of the brand’s ethos. “We wanted to display a mix of historical pieces, iconic pieces, and recent pieces,” explains Christophe Caillaud, the CEO of Liaigre. “This display creates an environment that is very close to what our inspiration for a space in India would look like.”

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/68dbc7b9ae8bc385db05c81b]|||*The Galet bookcase with movable parts.*|||

[#image: /photos/68dbc48606dacd007b238db4]|||*The structure of the ochre ⁠Basse Terre II Sofa was intended to be cubical yet comfortable.*|||

+++

*Also read: [Christian Liaigre’s first home in India is a contemporary take on the traditional bungalow](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/magazine-story/influences-from-mughal-architecture-and-indian-palaces-meld-inside-this-charming-delhi-home/#s-cust0){: target="_blank"}*

Indeed, that is one of the core tenets of Liaigre as a brand— to fit the context of the space they are in, and work with clients to create something that is fit for their home. Some of the highlights of the show include the Galet bookcase—which means pebble in French. The Galet is a stunning library piece that has moving ‘pebbles’ fitted between the shelves, adding a playful element to the design. Right next to the library is a signature lounge chair that is not on display in Liaigre showrooms anymore. This historic piece is the Latin daybed, and displays exquisite workmanship with its double stitching.



[#image: /photos/68dbc389af7ca2bdf1d6b5db]|||*The living room set up features the Sienne floor lamp in the far left corner, the iconic wooden Nagato stool in front of the mirror, and the bronze Citron lamp adorns the side table near the Augustin sofa. The composition is grounded by the Saint-Germain armchairs in the foreground.*|||

“Of course, we could not display Liaigre without showing some of our pieces in bronze,” shares Caillaud, talking about the iconic Saint-Germain armchairs designed by Christian Liaigre himself. The structure is cast in bronze, and has a certain sculptural quality that is reminiscent of artist Alberto Giacometti’s work. Another iconic piece that forms the living area of the exhibition is the Augustin sofa. This is also one of Christian Liaigre’s own designs, and is the first of its kind to introduce low, casual seating. The structure of it is still very formal, yet the seating allows for easy comfort and conversations.

*Also read: [Christian Liaigre’s interiors for Vertigo up the ante for superyacht style](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/content/christian-liaigres-interiors-for-vertigo-up-the-ante-for-superyacht-style/){: target="_blank"}*

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/68dbc916ae8bc385db05c81f]|||*The dining room features a mix of formal and informal seating.*|||

[#image: /photos/68dbca60ae8bc385db05c821]|||*This outdoor section features the darker iteration of the Naja table created by Eric Schmitt for Liaigre.*|||

+++

As we move to the dining area of the exhibition, the brand’s philosophy of comfort, craftsmanship, and contrast coalesces. A large formal [dining table](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/dining-table-vastu-8-major-red-flags-to-watch-out-for/){: target="_blank"} is illuminated by signature lighting above, yet the seating that surrounds it is casual— a mix of benches and chairs. Here too the contrast comes out, with the textured wooden backrest of the chairs complimenting the smooth leather seating of the bench.

The most striking throughline that connects Liaigre to India is a deep reverence for craftsmanship. Each piece is crafted to perfection, with many subtle details in texture and material shining through upon closer inspection. Many Liaigre collections are made in collaboration with seminal artists like Eric Schmitt, Karen Swami and others.

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/68dbca611d135afd3bfd79ad]|||*The outdoor section of the showcase is anchored by the Craie table by Karen Swami. It features a stoneware base and an oakwood top.*|||

[#image: /photos/68dbcaf21d135afd3bfd79af]|||*This section of the showcase has a more relaxed and casual atmosphere.*|||

+++

“I think our way of working with interior architecture and furniture is very compatible with the quality of the architecture that we have found in India,” Caillaud explains, “we are excited to continue this dialogue between our craftsmanship and [Indian craftsmanship](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/7-standout-pieces-from-ads-craftsmanship-issue-that-celebrate-the-art-of-handmade-design/){: target="_blank"}.” Liaigre is eager to meet the Indian design community, whether that means taking temporary showcases such as this to other cities, or opening a permanent showroom in Mumbai itself, the possibilities are endless. “At the end of the day, we don't want to be the biggest company, but we do want to be the closest to our clients,” concludes Christophe Caillaud.

*The showcase is on display at Æquo in Mumbai until the 20th of October.*

title: Solaya, an Exclusive New Development in Dubai, Is a New Benchmark in Beachfront Living
content:
Set against the shimmering backdrop of the Arabian Gulf, Jumeirah 1 is one of Dubai’s most sought-after neighbourhoods — known for its pristine beaches, upscale dining, retail offerings, and elegant waterfront homes.

The latest addition to the neighbourhood is Solaya, an exclusive new development of 234 residences, spanning nine buildings set across 40 acres.

Solaya is the latest result of the fruitful partnership between Dubai Holding Investments and Brookfield Properties, and is part of the Meraas portfolio, which also includes Bvlgari Residences, Bluewaters Residences and Nikki Beach Residences.



[#image: /photos/68e3bfd051eaad537e04b95c]

The beauty of the development’s breathtaking setting will be matched by its design. The world-renowned architecture studio Foster \+ Partners, whose projects include iconic buildings such as the Gherkin in London and the Hearst Tower in New York, has designed the buildings, while the interiors are created by 1508 London, a firm known for its timeless and elegant aesthetic and meticulous attention to detail.

The residences range from two to five bedrooms, and include a collection of spectacular penthouses with private lobbies and large terraces with infinity pools, as well as 18 garden houses with courtyard gardens and private pools, and duplexes with large terraces. Each is designed to support a connection to nature and the sense of well-being that brings, with airy, open-plan living spaces, high ceilings, panoramic views of the city and skyline, and a layout that allows seamless indoor-outdoor flow.

To answer an increased demand for the highest quality wellness facilities in modern developments, Solaya will include a spa with treatment rooms and therapeutic saunas, and a state-of-the-art fitness centre. Other leisure facilities such as a private cinema, private dining rooms, a residents’ lounge and beautifully designed outdoor spaces will offer opportunities for socialising and connection, and ensure residents can maintain a healthy and fulfilling lifestyle.

Outside the development itself, the full range of premium lifestyle amenities for which Dubai is renowned is close at hand: both J1 Beach, with its world-class restaurants and beach clubs, and Downtime Dubai, with its vibrant mix of cultural and lifestyle experiences, are within easy reach.



[#image: /photos/68e3bfcf51eaad537e04b958]

“Solaya reflects our unwavering commitment to collaborating with world-class partners to create extraordinary destinations that elevate well-being and foster connections with nature – values that are increasingly sought-after by today’s discerning homeowners,” says Khalid Al Malik, Chief Executive Officer of Dubai Holding Real Estate. “As consumer preferences continue to shift towards spaces with wellness-focused design and thoughtfully curated environments, Solaya both meets and exceeds these aspirations by introducing a new standard of residential living within one of the most established and desirable communities in Dubai.”

“This rare opportunity offers the privilege of beachfront living in the heart of Jumeirah 1, alongside renowned dining and lifestyle destinations,” says Rob Devereux, Chief Executive Officer of Brookfield Properties. “Solaya embodies a refined balance of exclusivity, elegance and connection to the sea.”

With its unrivalled location, focus on design excellence and commitment to providing a refined and sophisticated lifestyle, Solaya is setting a new standard in sophisticated beachfront living.

*Discover Solaya — where beachfront living meets timeless elegance. Visit [solaya.meraas.com](https://solaya.meraas.com/){: target="_blank"} to register your interest.*

title: Una casa in stile Craftsman si trasforma in una residenza moderna e originale con vista panoramica su Los Angeles
content:
**Una casa in stile Craftsman rivive nell'equilibrio tra passato glorioso ed elegante modernità senza rinunciare a una vista panoramica su Los Angeles.**

Materiali naturali, dettagli artigianali di pregio e tratti orizzontali che accarezzano il paesaggio fondendosi senza sforzo con il panorama. Una casa in [stile **Craftsman**](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/questa-casa-a-los-angeles-in-stile-craftsman-e-letteralmente-immersa-nel-bosco/) è semplice e funzionale, lontana dalle case vittoriane, così finemente decorate. I colori esaltano le tonalità neutre della terra - marroni, beige e grigi -, con accenti di nero, verde o blu perfetti per sottolineare un’estetica semplice e lineare, spesso ripresa anche per gli arredi interni. Quando il team di [**OWIU Design**](https://www.owiu-design.com/){: target="_blank"} ha deciso di lavorare al progetto **Cazador** si è trovato di fronte un edificio di questo genere, e la richiesta del cliente è stata chiara fin da subito: senza stravolgere l’esistente, il nuovo proprietario desiderava un *buen retiro* immerso nella pace di **Glassell Park, [Los Angeles](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/casa-los-angeles-murales-mar-vista/)**. La prima cosa che ha colpito **Amanda Gunawan** e **Joel Wong**, i fondatori dello Studio, è stata la posizione molto particolare di questa casa a [Los Angeles](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/villa-los-angeles-tranquilla-atmosfera-anni-sessanta/). Arroccata su una collina, Cazador gode di una splendida vista, a loro parere non del tutto valorizzata. «La ristrutturazione doveva mantenere la sensazione di intimo calore già proprio della casa, ma era necessario aprire gli spazi e privilegiare la vista panoramica».



[#image: /photos/68ea7f91f1a79c6c145d38f5]

## Un colpo di genio
Il layout originale, tipicamente Craftsman Style, era caratterizzato da stanze segmentate e dalla [luce naturale](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/luce-naturale-casa-galleria-dallessenza-borghese-roma/) insufficiente. Occorreva intervenire prima di tutto della disposizione degli ambienti e trasformare gli spazi suddivisi e angusti in un open space arioso, per accedere al terrazzo da ogni angolo della casa. «Abbiamo risolto il problema ampliando lo spazio tra cucina e [living](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/soggiorno-moderno-piu-belli-ad-italia-idee-da-copiare/), creando un ambiente arioso che massimizza abitabilità e luce. Gli adeguamenti strutturali, come l’uso di colonne che suggeriscono una divisione senza ostacolare l’illuminazione, hanno preservato il passato architettonico».



[#image: /photos/68ea7f9e574abb5572130415]

Si è rivelata subito vincente la scelta di ripensare le tre camere da letto: «la ristrutturazione ha rivisto spazi in precedenza suddivisi, invertendo la camera da letto principale e quella degli ospiti, un passaggio che consente di sfruttare al meglio lo scenario naturale. L’aggiunta di nuovi materiali e [finiture](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/casa-collina-dettagli-artigianali-finiture-pregio/), bilanciano carattere antico e calma contemporanea».



[#image: /photos/68ea7faef1a79c6c145d38f7]



[#image: /photos/68ea8033f4af52fac9386c34]

Chiaro che l’intervento di OWIU Design ha regalato una nuova identità, più contemporanea, a tutti gli ambienti. L’ingresso si spalanca su una zona giorno luminosa che evidenzia da subito la trasformazione. Tutto ruota attorno alla [cucina](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/tendenze-cucina-2025-nuovi-colori-verde-mocha-mousse/), incorniciata da soffitti a volta e colonne. I materiali naturali, le [texture](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/duplex-new-york-design-classico-colori-texture-foto/) tenui e i toni caldi del legno creano un’atmosfera tranquilla e accogliente. Il living si collega al terrazzo, sfumando i confini tra *indoor* e *outdoor*.



[#image: /photos/68ea7fbdf4af52fac9386c30]



[#image: /photos/68ea804bf4af52fac9386c36]

## La cucina, il nuovo punto di riferimento
Situata al centro della casa, la cucina è il punto di riferimento spaziale ed emotivo. Spiccano i mobili in acciaio inossidabile della serie **Jean Nouvel Reflect** di **Reform**, scelti per la superficie riflettente che amplifica la luce solare. I ripiani in quarzo aggiungono luminosità e resistenza, mentre gli [elettrodomestici](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/bonus-elettrodomestici-novita-prodotti-forno-aspirapolvere-frigorifero/) integrati e i dettagli dalle linee pulite confermano l’enfasi dello Studio su funzionalità e forma. La zona pranzo si protende naturalmente verso l’esterno, disegnando una zona di intrattenimento in cui è piacevole trascorrere del tempo in compagnia.



[#image: /photos/68ea80f2fb6e13f4de62314a]

+++pullquote

> «Cazador incarna appieno la raffinata filosofia del nostro Studio: l’architettura come riflesso di consapevolezza, precisione e calma»

Amanda Gunawan e Joel Wong

+++

La “nuova” camera da letto principale dimostra uno splendido colpo d’occhio sulla collina, esaltando il legame di tutto l’edificio con il paesaggio circostante. Le pareti in [intonaco veneziano](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/villa-australia-stile-modernista-piscina/) applicato a mano e i soffitti angolari aggiungono una qualità morbida e tattile alla stanza. Arredi minimalisti, toni neutri e semplici tendaggi rafforzano l’estetica serena e tranquilla che caratterizza Cazador.



[#image: /photos/68ea7fe4f1a79c6c145d38f9]

> +++group-2
> 
> [#image: /photos/68ea809afb6e13f4de623148]
> 
> [#image: /photos/68ea8087bf21959353f3dc9b]
> 
> +++

Per gli ospiti, poi, una gradita sorpresa: la camera a loro dedicata ha ricevuto lo stesso livello di attenzione, con finiture in stucco veneziano personalizzate e un’attenta rivisitazione dell’architettura già esistente. I [soffitti inclinati](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/dipingere-soffitto-tendenza-2025-idee-consigli/) non sono stati trattati come limiti strutturali ma come elementi di design da valorizzare, creando spazi scultorei realmente invitanti. Un *fil rouge* che si estende anche ai bagni, improntati a un **design minimalista**, strutturato e molto *light*. L’uso di materiali riflettenti e naturali bilancia funzionalità e relax.



[#image: /photos/68ea7ffff4af52fac9386c32]

*Last but not least*, eccoci all’ampia terrazza e il [giardino sul retro](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/progettare-un-giardino-6-regole-oro-per-uno-spazio-outdoor-da-sogno/). Entrambi sono estensioni degli spazi abitativi interni. Il terrazzo offre un ampio spazio per pranzare e rilassarsi, mentre l'area verde sul retro presenta un invitante sentiero e superfici in [granito](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/villa-hawaii-ristrutturazione-elegante-luce-legno/), progettato sia per intrattenimenti informali che per momenti di tranquilla riflessione. Soddisfatti del risultato? Il team di OWIU Design non ha dubbi: «Cazador incarna appieno la raffinata filosofia dello Studio: l’architettura come riflesso di consapevolezza, precisione e calma, in questo caso trasformando una residenza in un originale rifugio in una città caotica come Los Angeles».



[#image: /photos/68ea8014bf21959353f3dc99]

### Leggi anche:
- [Da casa tradizionale a paradiso mediterraneo a picco sul mare: ecco come una casa nelle isole Eolie è diventata un gioiello minimal di luce e relax](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/mare-casa-a-filicudi-terrazze-eolie-giulia-foscari/){: target="_blank"}
- [In un edificio firmato da Caccia Dominioni a Milano, l’elegante ristrutturazione di questo appartamento di 70 mq è una vera lezione di stile a cui ispirarsi](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/ristrutturazione-elegante-appartamento-milano-70-metri-quadri-caccia-dominioni/){: target="_blank"}
- [Chi l’avrebbe mai detto? Questo appartamento appartiene a una coppia over 85. E, forse, l’amore per il design è il loro segreto più grande](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/appartamento-80-metri-quadri-coppia-over-85-stile-mediterraneo-contemporaneo/){: target="_blank"}
- [Da villino anonimo a casa su misura: Villa Riparbella ridefinisce il concetto di casa vacanze con un pavimento “magico”](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/villa-riparbella-piscina-casa-vacanze-pavimento-magico-studio-benaim/){: target="_blank"}
- [Quattro cottage in Canada, rifugio ideale per chi ama la natura, gli animali e i venti impetuosi dell’Oceano](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/cottage-canada-rifugio-natura-oceano/)

title: I 3 consigli rivoluzionari di un architetto per ristrutturare un piccolo appartamento
content:
**Ristrutturare un piccolo appartamento, 3 consigli rivoluzionari da un architetto.**

Nel cuore del [Marais](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/appartamento-marais-cube-architectes-minimalismo-raffinatezza/){: target="_blank"}, **LIAO Design ha affrontato con successo la delicata sfida di combinare storia e modernità**, serenità e raffinatezza in questa ristrutturazione luminosa e armoniosa. Ecco i consigli per ristrutturare un piccolo appartamento prendendo spunto da questo [piccolo](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/piccolo-appartamento-xviii-secolo-marais-liao-design-storia-modernita/){: target="_blank"} spazio nel celebre quartiere parigino.

“Ho comprato questo piccolo appartamento nel Marais, in un edificio del XVIII secolo, per il suo carattere... E perché era tutto da rifare”, dice ridendo [**JiaHao Liao, fondatore di LIAO Design**](https://www.jiahaoliao.com/fr){: target="_blank"}. Fin dalla sua prima visita, l’interior designer aveva in mente una sfida: **restituire a questo [vecchio](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/come-trasformare-vecchio-fienile-casa-ristrutturazione/){: target="_blank"} appartamento l’autenticità dei volumi originali**. Più che una semplice ristrutturazione, si tratta di una trasformazione radicale, resa ancora più ambiziosa dai vincoli imposti dai soffitti bassi.

## Rendere il soffitto verticale se il soffitto è basso
### 
### Installare persiane a listelle


[#image: /photos/68dcf9d9b89c4017b53c765e]|||Tre grandi finestre illuminano la stanza quando le persiane a lamelle sono ripiegate. Sopra un divano e un tavolino in metallo (Kave Home), un dipinto di Roland Bouvier. Di fronte, tavolini in legno (AM.PM) e poltrone (NV Gallery).|||

“L’unico problema di questo appartamento era **l’altezza del soffitto di soli 2,15-2,20 metri**. Per questo motivo ho aggiunto un po’ di verticalità con le persiane interne, le coperture dei termosifoni e le porte degli armadi per aumentarla visivamente”, spiega JiaHao Liao. **Per questo è stato scelto il legno**. Nel soggiorno, le persiane e i paraventi interni conferiscono all’appartamento il suo fascino. “L’idea era quella di aggiungere elementi verticali e, in un soggiorno, non pensavo fosse necessario avere tende che bloccassero la luce come è necessario in una camera da letto”, continua l’architetto.

### Estendere la stessa idea all’armadio


[#image: /photos/68dcf9d9b89c4017b53c7662]|||Riprendendo il tema delle listelle in legno delle persiane, gli armadi della camera da letto principale aggiungono un’ariosa verticalità e un'elegante geometria alla stanza. L’accostamento dei materiali si esprime al meglio con il marmo del camino originale, il parquet chiaro a spina di pesce (Décoplus Parquets) e la pittura a calce (Arcane Industries) che riveste pareti e soffitti. Letto e comodini (Kave Home). Specchi (NV Gallery). Biancheria da letto (Westwing).|||

Nella camera da letto principale, gli armadi riprendono il tema delle listelle in legno delle persiane. Questo conferisce alla stanza una verticalità ariosa e una geometria elegante.

***
## Una scrivania estraibile con doppia funzione
+++group-2

[#image: /photos/68dcf9d9b89c4017b53c7664]|||La stanza è illuminata da una grande finestra di vetro sporgente, come un bow window. Grandi tende (Tapisserie Bulard et Cie) su binari (Odec) tengono fuori la luce di notte.|||

[#image: /photos/68dcf9d9b89c4017b53c7665]|||Una scrivania pieghevole crea quando è necessario un comodo spazio di lavoro. Lampada (Venture Home).|||

+++

L’interior designer ha creato una scrivania ribaltabile che conferisce a questa piccola stanza una **duplice funzione di camera degli ospiti/spazio di lavoro**. “Mi piace la funzionalità del progetto e la possibilità di creare spazi modulari. Questa soluzione evita di introdurre una scrivania che avrebbe occupato troppo spazio”. Una scrivania pieghevole si inserisce in un telaio che si integra con i contenitori tecnici e funge anche da cornice per la porta.

***
## Trasformare i limiti in idee geniali
### 
### Una panca giapponese che nasconde


[#image: /photos/68dcf9d9b89c4017b53c765c]|||In cucina, il laminato in simil-rovere testurizzato e venato riveste i mobili della cucina in una morbida monocromia. Le sedie in legno e corda (set Kave Home) circondano un tavolo le cui gambe riprendono il motivo verticale a listelli delle persiane. In alto, una lampada a sospensione (Audo Copenhagen). Sulla parete destra, un dipinto di Véronique Bigo. Grande vaso (Flora Déco).|||

Allo stesso modo, in cucina, una lunga panca a pavimento in [stile giapponese](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-stile-giapponese-feng-shui-hong-kong/){: target="_blank"} è nata dall’esigenza di nascondere una struttura metallica di rinforzo: “Questo piccolo gradino su cui appoggiare gli oggetti nasce dalla decisione di non livellare il pavimento e perdere ancora più spazio. Funziona bene, come sempre, il vincolo dà origine alla creazione”.

### Il bow window come piattaforma


[#image: /photos/68dcf9d9b89c4017b53c7666]|||Questo enorme bow window in stile galleria conduce ai contenitori tecnici. JiaHao Liao ha rielaborato la parte inferiore della finestra con un vetro scanalato per conferirgli un aspetto più verticale.|||

JiaHao Liao ha aggiunto un **pannello di vetro scanalato alla grande finestra originale della vecchia cucina**, aggiungendo verticalità e una sobria raffinatezza che è la firma di questo progetto. Questo immenso bow window, costruito come una piattaforma in stile galleria, conduce a spazi tecnici di stoccaggio di cui non si sospetterebbe mai l’esistenza.

Articolo originale pubblicato su *AD [Francia](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/astuces-revolutionnaires-architecte-renover-petit-appartement){: target="_blank"}*, adattato da Paola Corazza.

### Leggi anche:
- [Omaggio a Giorgio Armani, maestro del design, nel giorno dei funerali: le sue ultime parole ad](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/giorgio-armani-design-intervista-ad/){: target="_blank"}
- [Un piccolo, piccolissimo appartamento di 15 m2 pieno di luce sui tetti di Montmartre](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/piccolo-appartamento-15-m2-luce-tetti-montmartre-parigi-helene-lacombe/){: target="_blank"}
- [Questa casa a Los Angeles è una dichiarazione d’amore al colore: dal rosso ciliegia al verde chiaro, fino al blu cobalto](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-los-angeles-leah-ring-stanze-colorate-abbinamenti/#intcid=_ad-it-right-rail_0c82d64e-005e-4e67-9a57-fb641c9fad47_popular4-2){: target="_blank"}
- [Nel suo pied-à-terre a Parigi (57 m2) la fondatrice di Ghia ha eliminato le piastrelle rosa e scelto il marmo color cioccolato](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/pied-a-terre-parigi-57-m2-fondatrice-ghia-marmo-color-cioccolato/#intcid=_ad-it-right-rail_0c82d64e-005e-4e67-9a57-fb641c9fad47_popular4-2){: target="_blank"}
- [Una piccola casa da sogno a Milano: 35 metri quadri che, nonostante le dimensioni ridotte, si rivelano sorprendentemente generosi in termini di funzionalità](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/piccola-casa-milano-35-metri-funzionalita-materiali-pregio-archventil/){: target="_blank"}
- [Questo appartamento di 79 m2 a Brooklyn Heights è il massimo del romanticismo](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/appartamento-a-brooklyn-heights-romantico-interni-talia-mayden/){: target="_blank"}

title: Una casa estilo retro evoca armonía entre tonos cálidos envolventes
content:
**Un estilo retro con un toque moderno y distintivo: así puede definirse la inspiración que dio vida a esta antigua residencia situada en Milán**.

La casa della Meridiana es una joya arquitectónica de finales de la década de 1920, obra de Giuseppe de Finetti, enclavada en el tranquilo laberinto de calles del barrio de San Calimero. Basta con alzar la vista para descubrir su belleza que se revela en la fachada.

“Me impresionó la distribución”, comenta Cecilia Avogadro, a quien los nuevos propietarios encargaron la tarea de reimaginar esta **vivienda de 320 metros cuadrados** situada en la tercera planta, para adaptarla a la actualidad, buscando el equilibrio entre obsecuencia y libertad.



[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e663]

## Una restauración delicada
“Una vida viajando por el mundo y luego la elección de Milán: habían visto más de 90 casas, buscaban ‘la indicada’”, comienza narrando el diseñador. Avogadro, junto con su colega Paul Abbiati, estudió los dibujos originales de de Finetti, restauró las puertas y recuperó las inusuales manillas en forma de huevo, rehaciéndolas en una fundición. “También conservamos la gran chimenea de mármol negro, una cantera que, según me dijeron, ya está agotada. Al parecer es la única que se conserva”.



[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e664]

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e665]

[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e666]

+++

## El espíritu del hogar milanés de los años 20
**Pero la reforma vino acompañada de una revolución inspirada en el estilo retro.** La distribución se modificó por completo para crear tres salas de estar, una cocina con zona de televisión contigua y tres dormitorios con baño. “Los clientes insistieron en **eliminar el estilo palladiano** **histórico** que les resultaba demasiado sobrio”. El diseñador decidió entonces complacerlos sin perder la esencia del estilo, y los llevó al Laboratorio Morseletto en Vicenza, donde seleccionaron [piedras y mármoles](https://www.admagazine.com/editors-pick/cuarzo-rosa-todo-lo-que-debes-saber-sobre-la-piedra-del-amor-20210414-8380-articulos){: target="_blank"} amarillos y rojos para elaborar artesanalmente un estilo palladiano para la entrada, un suelo de terrazo veneciano en las salas de estar y un elegante acabado arlequín en la cocina.



[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e662]

Un hilo conductor armonioso y un metrónomo espacial. El diseñador lo transforma todo, pero parece que siempre estuvo allí. Como el ventanal de la cocina, que, gracias a un permiso afortunado, ahora lo envuelve por completo, desapareciendo y **creando una veranda en la fachada de la** **casa estilo retro**. Futurismo sofisticado. “La vista es preciosa, con vistas a San Calimero, el convento y los edificios de los años 60”.

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e667]

[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e668]

+++

## Sabor y espíritu internacionales
Avogadro saborea cada detalle visualmente y deja que le susurren su camino: telas Dedar, una pasta texturizada de peso medio para las paredes de la sala, una [escultural cocina](https://www.admagazine.com/articulos/como-renovar-tu-cocina-en-departamento-rentado){: target="_blank"} diseñada a medida con paneles que presentan un diseño de Ettore Sottsass para Alpi, separada por una cortina de fieltro que se abre hacia la sala. “La pareja quería un ambiente internacional; **esta casa estilo retro está siempre abierta a los huéspedes**, pero me di cuenta de que la atmósfera de finales de los años 20 que percibí aquí la primera vez ya era internacional”, continúa. “Trabajé mucho con Cini Boeri, y para ella, los muebles a medida funcionaban mejor, así que suelo diseñar piezas personalizadas. Pero a la hora de **añadir elementos decorativos, no niego que me permito remontarme al menos un siglo atrás**. Por ejemplo, muebles escandinavos de los años 30 —Hoffmann, Aalto, el estilo gris sueco—, pero también Albini y un aparador belga de los años 60. Quería crear un diálogo entre los límites del espacio y el tiempo”.



[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e669]



[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e66a]

## Ambientes cálidos para una casa representativa
**El resultado es una casa señorial que hace guiño al estilo retro**, pero donde nada es meramente decorativo. La mesa de la cocina, diseñada a medida, puede combinarse con el escritorio, y las dos salas alternan entre [ambientes más cálidos](https://www.admagazine.com/articulos/colores-de-otono-para-el-hogar-y-como-combinarlos-correctamente){: target="_blank"}: uno con sofás rojos y una alfombra de piel de poni, y otros más frescos en tonos azul celeste con una alfombra floral que también sirve de habitación para invitados cuando es necesario. Al fin y al cabo, esa puerta de hierro del recibidor suele permanecer abierta.



[#image: /photos/68ffcb71ff9867963f77e66b]

*Artículo publicado originalmente en* **AD *Italia*.**

title: Les 4 règles d'une experte pour un bureau organisé — et les erreurs à ne pas commettre
content:
[#image: /photos/6901d83a705b433606413af4]

Dans un [bureau](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/tendances-bureau-ideal-chez-soi), l’ordre influence directement la productivité. Un espace bien pensé permet notamment de gagner du temps pour trouver ses fournitures et ses dossiers. Par un procédé de rangements, de catégorisation et de tri régulier, l’objectif est simple : s’y retrouver. Certains trouvent l’ordre dans le célèbre « fouilli organisé ». D’autres, en revanche, cherchent à faire de leur espace de travail un lieu structuré. Il ne s’agit pas là d’une question de beau, mais d’optimisation de sa créativité et de son inspiration. Dans ce cas, comment trouver le bon compromis entre esthétique et fonctionnel dans ses rangements ? Conseils d’une [professionnelle du rangement](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/rangement-cuisine-dees-organisees), Claudia Friio.

## Les trois erreurs les plus fréquentes dans son espace de travail
La principale source de désordre sur un bureau naît **des objets sans place définie**. « *Que ce soit sur le mobilier ou dans les tiroirs, les piles de papiers, factures et documents finissent par s’empiler et traîner n’importe où. Niveau papeterie, ce sont les stylos qui sont mal rangés et répartis aux quatre coins de la pièce* ». D’autres effets n’ont pas de place assignée dans la maison et se retrouvent sur le bureau par défaut. « *Lorsque l’on possède un trop-plein de câbles, de gadgets, effets électroniques ou simples pièces de monnaies, souvent, ces affaires terminent sur le bureau. Il est, comme le tiroir de la commode de [l’entrée](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/idees-decoration-entree), une "zone chaude”. Cela signifie qu’on y relaie tout ce dont on ne sert pas pour le travail, mais l’y range tout de même. Souvenez-vous, un espace de travail ne doit pas devenir un lieu “fourre-tout”.»*

En seconde erreur, **le manque de solutions de rangement adaptées.** « *Certaines n’ont pas les bonnes dimensions. Des placards sont trop profonds pour ce que vous avez besoin de stocker et engendrent une perte de place considérable dans la pièce. Aussi, des bureaux manquent de classeurs, de pochettes ou de lutins. »*

Enfin, une **organisation non pensée en amont** assure un [espace de travail](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/comment-amenager-bureau-petit-espace) encombré. « *Pensez à la manière dont vous allez travailler. Sinon, votre espace ne sera pas adapté. Par exemple, posséder seulement un beau bureau face à un mur est, certes, esthétique, mais manque cruellement de place. Résultat, vous n’y travaillez pas. Vous vous installez sur la table de la salle à manger et finissez par accumuler le bazar sur ce petit bureau qui n’en est plus un.* »

## La meilleure disposition pour un bureau agréable
Ne négligez pas l’endroit ni la manière dont vous placez votre bureau. Si vous n’avez pas la possibilité de lui dédier une [pièce](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/pieces-revolutionne-histoire-design-redaction-ad), « *tout dépend de là où vous vous sentez le mieux chez vous. Le lieu doit être agréable à vivre. C’est notamment la raison pour laquelle le bureau se trouve souvent dans le séjour, ou la chambre si vous en avez la place.* » Pour cause, « *le mobilier en lui-même, si vous le pouvez, **doit être grand**. Un support large évite de vous y sentir étriqués* ». Enfin, et de manière indispensable, « ***positionnez le bureau face à une vue dégagée, idéalement devant une fenêtre**. Travailler face à l’extérieur ou à minima sur votre [intérieur](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/decoration-chambre-adulte-tendance) change drastiquement la donne comparé à une session de travail face à un mur. »*



[#image: /photos/6901d83a705b433606413af4]

+++pullquote

> *« Également, **évitez qu’une porte se trouve dans votre dos** pour une atmosphère plus sereine. Une fois installés, ayez une bonne assise, un bon éclairage et une poubelle à proximité pour allier confort et praticité.* »

+++



[#image: /photos/6901d83a705b433606413af3]

Dans une [petite pièce](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/petits-espaces-triangle-or-parisien), même s’il sera face au mur, « *les bureaux muraux et à tiroirs restent une bonne alternative* ».

## Les solutions de rangements les plus efficaces pour un bureau fonctionnel
### 
### Le mobilier répartit les affaires par fréquence d’utilisation
Pour se créer un espace de travail à la maison, « *l’essentiel est d’y trouver un **secrétaire avec tiroirs** et un **caisson de rangements**. Ces premiers éléments doivent conserver ce dont vous avez besoin à disposition. Pour les classeurs de documents administratifs que vous conservez plus par précaution, ayez un **[placard à étagères](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/idees-placards-entree-architectes-interieur)** dédié. Rangez-y ce que vous utilisez moins régulièrement, mais qui vous reste utile.* » En l’absence de tiroirs intégrés au bureau, des meubles sur roues existent et s’y glissent en dessous. « *Si vous optez pour une desserte, veillez à ce qu’elle ne soit pas trop chargée. Ce petit mobilier à roulettes est tendance mais n’a pas de placards fermés. S’il est mal organisé, vous créez un bazar visuel*. »



[#image: /photos/6901d83aa0e816e8658ca9aa]

### Les accessoires de tri et de rangements ordonnent la papeterie
Pour ce qui est des fournitures, l’objectif est de les ordonner sans encombrer visuellement. « *Comme dans toutes les pièces de la maison, **pensez à des solutions de [rangements fermés](https://www.admagazine.fr/adinspiration/article/6-rangements-astucieux-chambre-petit-espace)**. Une bannette à papier fermée, des organisateurs sous forme de tiroirs miniatures ou des trieurs à papiers dissimulent ce qui, sans structure, s’éparpille. Prenez l’exemple des enveloppes. Même si elles sont petites, ayez l’habitude de les ranger ainsi que le courrier dans ce type de rangements qui se placent sur le bureau. Le réflexe est de les glisser dans un tiroir par dessus des fournitures. Seulement ensuite, vous ne distinguez plus son contenu. »*



[#image: /photos/6901d83a7a2429d0321dfc31]

À l’intérieur même des tiroirs, **les organisateurs compartimentent** chaque [accessoire](https://www.admagazine.fr/decoration/shopping-deco/diaporama/beaux-et-utiles/62384) pour les identifier plus rapidement. *« Rangez-y tout ce qui est petit. Agrafeuse, ciseaux, petit carnet de note … vous gagnerez un temps précieux à ne plus avoir à fouiller dans le tiroir pour les trouver. »*



[#image: /photos/6901d83d705b433606413af6]

Pour **regrouper les câbles** qui encombrent le plus, « *des boîtes dédiées existent pour les dissimuler. Certains bureaux possèdent ce type de rangements intégrés à même leur structure. Ils sont alors dissimulés sur les côtés ou en dessous. Sinon, optez pour des attaches à clipser sur votre secrétaire pour que les chargeurs ne traînent plus au sol ou s’emmêlent.* »



[#image: /photos/6901d83aa0e816e8658ca9a9]

## Maintenir le bureau ordonné jour après jour
### 
### Désencombrer le bureau
+++pullquote

> « *La méthode pour gérer la papeterie et les piles qui s’accumulent est d’**identifier les catégories de papier** : Travail, santé, logement … Distinguez-les pour faciliter leur classement.* »

+++

**Choisissez ensuite les rangements en fonction des usages**. Les classeurs avec intercalaires offrent une meilleure prise en main des documents. À la différence des [boîtes](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/objets-brutalistes-moins-100-euros) ou des rangements verticaux, « *dont on ne peut pas distinguer à quoi correspond chaque papier sans devoir tous les retirer du contenant.* » **Dédiez également un endroit définitif pour le courrier entrant et en cours**. « *Un petit plateau à étages attitré à ces papiers est idéal. »*



[#image: /photos/6901d83a705b433606413af2]

Souvenez-vous : « *une place pour chaque chose et chaque chose à sa place. **En fin de journée, en une ou deux minutes, adoptez des [habitudes](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/plans-maison-evoluent-espaces-ouverts) simples et efficaces** : Replacez les objets utilisés dans la journée à leur place, triez les papiers à traiter et ceux à jeter. **Mensuellement, consacrez-vous à un tri plus en profondeur.*** »

### Connaître la vraie durée de conservation des papiers administratifs
N’accumulez pas inutilement. **Quotidiennement, « *traitez les papiers dès leur arrivée***_. Il existe une vraie problématique de procrastination sur la gestion de l’administratif. Si vous ne le gérez pas immédiatement, fixez vous à minima **une session hebdomadaire de consultation du courrier**. Sinon vous laissez traîner des dossiers et perdez un temps [précieux](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/decors-maxime-old-rouen)._ »

**Une fois par mois ou tous les quinze jours,** « *reprenez-les et classez-les. Ne gardez que ceux qui sont nécessaires. »* Nombreux conservent des factures ou documents confidentiels pendant dix ans. Cette période est la durée de leur conservation que l’on pense être minimum avant de pouvoir les jeter. Pourtant, elle est souvent bien plus courte que l’on ne le croirait. D’après les [recommandations du Service Public](https://www.service-public.gouv.fr/particuliers/vosdroits/F19134), **les papiers d’assurance** se conservent au moins 2 ans après la fin d’un contrat ou de la date du document. Ceux de la **banque**, comme un crédit, se gardent entre 2 à 5 ans. Les **impôts** et les taxes ; 3 ans après l’année d’imposition. Pour les factures de **logement**, jusqu’à 5 ans. Les ordonnances de **santé** et factures [d’hôpitaux](https://www.admagazine.fr/galerie/images-inquietantes-hopitaux-abandonnes), elles, ont une durée de conservation minimale de 1 à 4 ans.

En vérité, seuls les papiers d'**assurance vie**, les documents liés à un **dommage corporel**, un **DPE** (Diagnostic de Performance Énergétique) ou une facture relative à des t**ravaux importants** valent mieux d’être conservés 10 ans.

Quant aux papiers liés au **travail ou à la retraite**, ils se conservent jusqu’à la liquidation de la retraite, donc sans limite de temps par précaution *(\*Service Public)*

En conséquence, « ***chaque année, faites le point sur les papiers qui peuvent être jetés.** Vous serez content d’alléger vos classeurs. »*

title: Dubai Museum of Art: Everything We Know So Far About the Newest Landmark
content:
Dubai has unveiled plans for the Dubai Museum of Art (DUMA), a major [new cultural landmark](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/ameca-dubais-ai-robot-is-the-the-museum-of-the-futures-latest-employee){: target="_blank"} that will stand on the waters of Dubai Creek. Designed by Pritzker Prize-winning architect Tadao Ando, whose portfolio includes the Chichu Art Museum and the Bourse de Commerce in Paris, the project reflects the emirate’s ambition to strengthen its position as a global hub for creativity and cultural exchange.

“[Dubai](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/the-tallest-buildings-in-dubai-you-must-visit){: target="_blank"} has become a destination of choice for creatives from around the world, home to communities and industries working together to accelerate the growth of the creative economy,” said His Highness Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, who was in attendance during the project’s unveiling on October 26.



[#image: /photos/69034a58fee04ed82831d700]



[#externallink: /externallinks/65030450bb917946d989f4c2]

## Also Read: [Abu Dhabi’s Zayed National Museum Opens in December: Here Is Everything We Know About the Foster \+ Partners Designed Landmark](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/abu-dhabi-zayed-national-museum-opens-in-december-foster-partners-designed-landmark)
The launch was also attended by Her Highness Sheikha Latifa bint Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, Omar Al Futtaim, and other officials, underscoring the museum’s significance within the emirate’s cultural agenda. During the event, His Highness remarked that “culture and art are the soul of a city and a lens through which its progress is seen,” positioning the museum as both symbolic and strategic.

## Where will the Dubai Museum of Art be built?
The museum will be constructed on an artificial jetty that extends into Dubai Creek. Its surrounding landscape will feature a procession of palms, and a circular platform will give the impression that the building floats above the water.



[#image: /photos/69034a6833c27e586c305a65]

The five-storey structure is shaped like a curving shell inspired by the sea and the pearl, symbols of Dubai’s heritage. Smooth white panels fold and twist toward a glazed opening that captures desert light, while triangular apertures puncture the facade. At sunset, the surface absorbs amber tones, softening the engineered geometry into something atmospheric.

Inside, curved concrete walls frame galleries arranged around a central oculus that channels daylight downward in a pearlescent glow. Full-height glazing on the upper level offers views across the creek toward the city’s skyline, situating art within Dubai’s evolving urban context.

## Who is designing the Dubai Museum of Art?


[#image: /photos/69034a4743988bc4a50b411d]

“We are especially proud to have entrusted the design of DUMA to the world-renowned architect [Tadao Ando](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/tadao-ando-inside-hollywoods-favorite-architects-homes){: target="_blank"}, whose architecture speaks the language of light, silence, and soul,” said Omar Al Futtaim, Vice Chairman and CEO of Al-Futtaim Group. “In Dubai, his vision for the Museum of Art will stand as a serene dialogue between nature, water, and the human spirit, a masterpiece of restraint and emotion that embodies both ingenuity and timeless elegance, perfectly capturing the depth of his philosophy.”

Tadao Ando is one of the world’s most celebrated architects, known for a minimalist approach and masterful use of light and shadow. His work harmonises architecture and environment, with notable cultural projects including Japan’s Chichu Art Museum, the Modern Art Museum of Fort Worth in Texas, and the Bourse de Commerce in Paris.



[#article: /articles/661f712cac8f7ebd0e95d3ef]

Ando’s portfolio in the Middle East is still emerging; more than a decade ago, he was linked to the planned Maritime Museum on Saadiyat Island in Abu Dhabi, described by its developer at the time as a museum “[floating upon](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/the-worlds-biggest-yacht-turtle-shaped-boat-is-an-dollar8-billion-floating-city){: target="_blank"} a plane of water”, and his latest commission places him among the global architects shaping the UAE’s cultural landscape.

## What will Dubai’s Museum of Art offer?
“Dubai has become a destination of choice for creatives from around the world, home to communities and industries working together to accelerate the growth of the creative economy,” His Highness said on Dubai’s rapidly expanding creative sector. With its adaptable galleries, programming spaces and educational facilities, the Dubai Museum of Art is positioned to further cultivate this momentum.



[#image: /photos/69034a4bfee04ed82831d6fe]

Across the first and second floors, adaptable galleries will present modern and contemporary art. The third floor will host a restaurant and VIP lounge, while additional levels include event spaces suitable for artist talks, panel discussions, educational programmes, and art fairs designed to nurture emerging talent. The museum will also feature a library and study rooms to support future generations of creatives.

“Al-Futtaim remains dedicated to delivering this landmark with excellence, reflecting our enduring commitment to sustainability and the cultural sector’s vital role in shaping inclusive progress,” adds Omar Al Futtaim.

## When will Dubai’s Museum of Art open?
No official opening date has been announced. Press material states that no timeline for completion has been revealed yet, though the museum is expected to enrich the emirate’s art scene and further diversify its cultural offerings once complete. As the project progresses, further details about its programme, collection, and opening schedule are expected to be released.

It’s not the first time the UAE has paired bold cultural vision with landmark architecture: Jean Nouvel’s Louvre Abu Dhabi, [Frank Gehry’s Guggenheim Abu Dhabi](https://www.admiddleeast.com/gallery/most-awaited-new-museum-openings-of-2025-to-look-forward-to){: target="_blank"}, Zaha Hadid’s Sheikh Zayed Bridge — the list goes on. The Dubai Museum of Art arrives as the latest chapter in a national drive to create buildings that are as expressive as the collections they hold, amplifying the country’s architectural identity through global collaboration.



[#image: /photos/69034a7743988bc4a50b411f]



[#externallink: /externallinks/6503039e627979bdc04189a0]

title: Ce projet d'architectes spectaculaire affiche un toit végétal avec 6000 plantes
content:
**À Saint-Sébastien, le GOe Donostia mise sur un avenir vert pour la ville**

La bonne [architecture](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/styles-inconnus-geniaux-architecture-insolite) a toujours été comme la bonne cuisine : elles naissent toutes deux du dialogue entre des matériaux exquis, des techniques innovantes et, surtout aujourd’hui, d’approches capables de transformer notre environnement. Dedans comme dehors – car au fond, ces deux mondes n’ont-ils pas toujours été liés ? On le ressent en contemplant la mer, dans un jeu de saveurs et de formes, quand le geste du chef rejoint celui de l’architecte, du designer, de l’art sous toutes ses formes. On pourrait évoquer bien des lieux, mais tout cela se résume ici, au Gastronomy Open Ecosystem, ou GOe.

Le [projet](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/architecture-projets-fous-monde-2025) d’expansion du Basque Culinary Center à Saint-Sébastien témoigne du génie qui consiste à réinventer le tissu urbain à travers la recette parfaite : gastronomie, aménagement paysager et promesse tenue d’étendre une icône architecturale à son environnement grâce à une vision durable et innovante.

## GOe : cuisiner l’avenir durable de Saint-Sébastien


[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710c9f]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

Martin Berasategui, Pedro Subijana, Karlos Argiñano, Hilario Arbelaitz ou Eneko Atxa ne sont que quelques-uns des [chefs](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/duos-chefs-architectes-interieur) liés au Basque Culinary Center (BCC), une institution académique et de recherche créée à Saint-Sébastien en 2009 et composée de deux centres : la Faculté des sciences gastronomiques et le Centre de recherche et d’innovation. Il s’agit d’un projet de formation, de recherche et d’innovation axé sur le développement du secteur gastronomique et ses liens avec des domaines tels que la science, la [technologie](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/concilier-feng-shui-technologie) et, bien sûr, l’architecture.

C’est pourquoi l’évolution naturelle du Basque Culinary Center s’est ouverte à la ville à travers un nouvel écosystème, tel un nouveau phare architectural et durable de Saint-Sébastien. Car GOe est bien plus qu’un projet architectural : c’est une vision qui embrasse l’avenir depuis un environnement propice.

L’agence danoise [Bjarke Ingels Group](https://www.admagazine.fr/design/portraits/diaporama/rencontre-avec-le-starchitecte-bjarke-ingels/58481) (BIG) et le cabinet de conseil, d’architecture et de construction BAT, basé à Bilbao, ont conçu le bâtiment du BCC, situé dans le quartier de Miramon, ainsi qu’un second édifice, imaginé comme une extension architecturale du paysage urbain, dans le quartier de Zubiri-Manteo, à côté de la Maison de la culture Okendo. Ce nouvel espace de 9 000 mètres carrés crée un véritable pont entre science et gastronomie. Ici, on expérimente, on cherche, on innove et on enseigne, le tout dans un cadre respectueux de l’environnement, parfaitement intégré à son paysage.



[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710ca0]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

D’où son toit [végétalisé](https://www.admagazine.fr/adactualites/article/singapour-gratte-ciel-vegetalise-bjarke-ingels-80-000-plantes), conçu comme un prolongement naturel de la montagne dans le cadre du projet paysager GOe, réalisé par l’agence Lur Paisajistal, qui transforme le bâtiment en un espace écologique et biodiversifié, intégré au réseau vert urbain de la ville de Saint-Sébastien.

Il a été développé en coordination avec le service Parcs et Jardins de la mairie de Donostia-Saint-Sébastien, dans le cadre du Plan d’infrastructure verte et du Plan directeur des arbres de Donostia-Saint-Sébastien *« BOTANIKA »*, qui considère l’avenue Navarra comme un corridor vert fondamental reliant les quartiers d’Ategorrieta-Ulia, Egia et Gros à la [plage](https://www.admagazine.fr/galerie/9-plages-etranges-sublimes-monde).



[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710ca1]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

GOe agit comme un véritable connecteur vert, un pont entre la nature et la ville. Des espèces adaptées à l’environnement local y sont plantées. Elles introduisent davantage de nature dans l’espace urbain, augmentent la biodiversité et créent un cadre plus riche et plus sain pour les plantes, les animaux et les habitants. Le projet devient un nouveau repère écologique : il favorise la faune urbaine, renforce la connectivité verte et s’intègre harmonieusement au paysage, dans le respect des plans en vigueur. La couverture végétale du [bâtiment](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/10-concepts-comprendre-architecture) et la végétation des aménagements urbains viennent compléter cette démarche, formant une infrastructure écologique cohérente, diversifiée et fonctionnelle.



[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710ca2]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

Le toit végétalisé couvre 1 329 mètres carrés, ce qui en fait un véritable écosystème vivant intégré à l’architecture du bâtiment. Il accueille 6 216 plantes réparties en 26 espèces différentes : vivaces, arbustes et graminées, choisies pour leur faible besoin en [eau](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/plantes-interieur-necessitent-peu-eau), leur résistance au climat atlantique et leur intérêt tout au long de l’année.

La grande surface paysagée est structurée par des bandes d’arbustes persistants, sélectionnés pour leur feuillage durable et leur tolérance aux conditions salines. Ces bandes alternent avec cinq sections florales à floraison différenciée, qui offrent une richesse de couleurs et une diversité écologique continues : du blanc au bleu et violet, en passant par le jaune, sans oublier les plantes indigènes des milieux humides.



[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710ca3]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

L’alternance entre arbustes persistants et bandes de [plantes](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/grandes-plantes-interieur-decorateurs) vivaces crée une mosaïque végétale riche et dynamique. Les floraisons, les textures légères et les variations saisonnières renforcent l’esthétique du toit tout en favorisant la biodiversité : elles nourrissent les insectes pollinisateurs, offrent un refuge à la faune urbaine et proposent aux habitants une expérience végétale vivante et changeante.

Conçue avec l’entreprise basque Drenatura, la toiture fonctionne également comme un système de drainage durable. Elle collecte et réutilise l’eau de pluie grâce à un système d’irrigation passif, sans consommation d’énergie externe, réduisant ainsi l’usage de l’eau potable.

La végétation agit comme un filtre naturel et contribue à abaisser la [température](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/temperature-minimale-respecter-chez-soi-v2) de l’air de 2 à 5 degrés, atténuant l’effet *« îlot de chaleur »* dans le quartier de Manteo grâce à l’évaporation de l’eau de pluie.



[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710ca4]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

Cette toiture améliore non seulement la performance énergétique du bâtiment et contribue à la gestion durable de l’eau, mais elle s’ouvre également aux citoyens en tant qu’espace public actif. Les formes ondulées du bâtiment, conçues comme des [terrasses](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/idees-amenagement-terrasse) en gradins, créent une succession d’espaces en plein air qui relient le bâtiment à son environnement, estompant les frontières entre l’intérieur et l’extérieur. En transformant son toit en un paysage habitable, GOe prouve que l’architecture peut être à la fois un support écologique et un moteur de transformation urbaine.

Parallèlement, la végétation a été intégrée dans l’urbanisation complémentaire qui entoure le bâtiment, dont 2 271 mètres carrés correspondent à des espaces verts. Dans ces zones, 1 293 unités de 25 espèces différentes – [arbres](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/meilleurs-arbres-interieur-maison), arbustes et plantes vivaces – seront plantées, apportant biodiversité et valeur paysagère à l’environnement.



[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710ca5]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

Les espaces verts couvrent une superficie totale de 3 600 mètres carrés, ce qui renforce l’engagement en faveur de la durabilité et de l’intégration paysagère de l’environnement. Ensemble, elles constituent un réseau qui renforce la connectivité verte du quartier de Gros et met en valeur le rôle de l’avenue Navarra en tant que corridor naturel, puisque la voie a été configurée comme une bande végétale structurée qui se transforme en une tapisserie de textures et de [couleurs](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/couleurs-foncees-peindre-mur) tout au long de l’année et qui répond à des critères fonctionnels, écologiques et esthétiques, favorisant ainsi un paysage urbain plus résilient, accueillant et connecté aux écosystèmes naturels.

Quant au parc supérieur qui relie le bâtiment au quartier de Manteo, il constituera un parcours paysager de 2 200 mètres carrés. Dans cet environnement, des espèces adaptées au milieu côtier ont été utilisées, telles que des tamariniers ou des pins maritimes, conçues pour offrir de l’ombre, une diversité chromatique et une résistance au vent et à la salinité. En effet, toutes les mesures adoptées dans l’[aménagement](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/reduire-anxiete-amenagement-interieur) et le développement du projet paysager de GOe répondent également à la nécessité d’atténuer les effets du changement climatique et de s’y adapter, en apportant des solutions concrètes à partir de la conception urbaine.



[#image: /photos/69036f0def8faf3ca3710ca6]|||Avec l’aimable autorisation de GOe|||

Avec ce projet, GOe ne se contente pas de transformer un bâtiment en écosystème, mais propose un modèle d’infrastructure inspirant, capable de relever les défis environnementaux au cœur même de la ville. Sa conception est une réponse directe à l’urgence climatique et un engagement résolu en faveur d’espaces urbains plus biodiversifiés.

En intégrant la [nature](https://www.admagazine.fr/galerie/lieux-abandonnes-nature), le paysage et l’architecture, GOe démontre qu’il est possible de construire des environnements résilients qui améliorent la qualité de vie, renforcent les écosystèmes urbains et connectent les personnes à leur environnement.

Article initialement publié dans [*AD* Espagne](https://www.revistaad.es/articulos/bjarke-ingels-group-y-bat-firman-goe-donostia){: target="_blank"}.

title: 7 *AD*\-approved sofa designs that will still feel fresh in 2026
content:
In a design [landscape](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/10-architects-and-designers-in-surat-reimagining-the-built-landscape-with-purpose/){: target="_blank"} obsessed with what’s new, the most modern move is restraint. These timeless sofa designs endure because they prioritise proportion, texture, and tactility over trend—proof that true modernity never rushes.

## The Mid-Century Modern Couch: An Icon Reimagined


[#image: /photos/68cbb361915b28381cd498f4]|||*Pro-Tip: Add one curve nearby, a round coffee table or arc floor lamp balances the sofa’s linear geometry, as Studio Espaazo does in this Ahmedabad home. Keep the legs visible—exposed teak or metal legs create visual air and maintain that signature mid-century lightness.*|||

Born in post-war America, the [mid-century](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/this-multi-generational-villa-by-muselab-gives-the-motifs-of-kutch-a-mid-century-makeover/){: target="_blank"} modern sofa embodied a new kind of optimism: light, democratic, and human-scaled, when renowned designers such as Charles and Ray Eames, Florence Knoll, and George Nelson replaced heavy, carved furniture with clean horizontals, organic curves, and slender wooden legs. Eight decades on, its appeal endures because it balances form and function with quiet confidence: compact proportions which fit urban apartments, honest materials like teak, wool, or linen which age gracefully, and a geometry that harmonises with almost any style—from Scandinavian calm to Indian tropical modernism.

## The Sectional Sofa: For Stylish Ease


[#image: /photos/6863a2fe01e928c8f7030977]|||*Pro-Tip: As Concepto Studio does in this Bengaluru home, position the sectional away from walls; it looks sculptural and frames open-plan zones beautifully.*|||



[#image: /photos/6908518fe0d04a711165639b]|||*Pro-Tip: Rather than bulky end tables, opt for nested surfaces or built-in side ledges to complement the piece. Keep the side and centre tables low height, as seen in art collector Karan Bhagat’s Mumbai home—originally designed as a show flat by Rajiv Saini and reimagined by architect Siddharth Garimella.*|||

The sectional sofa has evolved from its suburban origins into a symbol of intelligent design—adaptable, sculptural, and unapologetically generous. Conceived in the mid-20th century to suit open layouts and shifting lifestyles, its genius lies in its modularity: each segment can transform a space, from an [intimate](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/a-designers-home-in-juhu-is-an-intimate-portrait-of-heritage-and-crafted-modernity-interior-designer-tania-shah/){: target="_blank"} reading corner to a sprawling social zone. Contemporary designers treat it as architecture rather than furniture—anchoring living areas, defining circulation, and softening modern geometries. With low profiles, tonal upholstery, and deep, sink-in seats, the sectional embodies relaxed luxury that doesn’t age. It’s as much a place to gather as it is to pause.

*Also read: [5 best sofa colours to breathe life into a neutral home](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/5-best-sofa-colours-to-breathe-life-into-a-neutral-home/){: target="_blank"}*

## The Modular Marvel: Where Form Meets Flexibility


[#image: /photos/683f428af160cc375552caa9]|||*Pro tip: With modular sofas, try to keep the palette monochrome to maintain visual calm. Let its sculptural form, not colour, create rhythm across the room.*|||

The modular sofa can be described as even a philosophy ofmodern living, beyond its life as furniture, and shares roots with the sectional sofa. Born in the late 1900s and popularised by icons like Mario Bellini’s *Camaleonda*, it challenged the static idea of a traditional couch by letting users compose their own configurations. Today, its appeal endures because it reflects how we actually inhabit our homes fluidly. Each unit can shift from intimate nook to social centrepiece with ease, embodying a kind of democratic design that feels both playful and poised. With soft edges, comfortable upholstery, and sculptural volume, the modular sofa is a versatile and [artistic](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/anish-kapoors-monte-santangelo-turns-everyday-commute-into-an-artistic-experience/){: target="_blank"} shape-shifter.

## The Bouclé Sofa: Elevating Texture


[#image: /photos/680f60f3d7037b1705713a37]|||*Pro-tip: Maintain the tones of the bouclé, as the texture works best in neutral, ivory or oat shades, as seen in this home by AT Studio. Pair it with tonal contrasts —dark wood, marble, or brushed metal—to ground its softness and keep the room from feeling too ethereal.*|||

The instantly recognizable bouclé sofa designs have achieved cult status for a reason—they turn simplicity into seduction. Once favoured by mid-century modernists for its soft tactility, *bouclé* (French for “looped yarn”) resurfaced in contemporary interiors as the textile of quiet luxury. Its tactile, cloud-like texture diffuses light, softens architectural lines, and invites touch—an antidote to the hard-edged [minimalism](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/in-this-5100-square-foot-bengaluru-penthouse-minimalism-gets-a-mediterranean-twist-pomtini-design-studio/){: target="_blank"} of recent decades. Designers now favour it not for novelty, but for its ability to balance sculptural form with comfort, making even the most minimal space feel warm and inhabited. Its versatility allows it to effortlessly find its place in all designs, from a pared-back Japandi apartment to a richly layered art deco interior. Whether curved, modular, or perched on slender legs, the bouclé sofa embodies modern, fluid comfort.

## The Reimagined Chesterfield: Tradition With A Twist


[#image: /photos/686e2c0bebca66233ec8ed90]|||*Pro-tip: A matte or suede finish modernises the traditional high-gloss look instantly, as seen in the library lounge of Goa’s Verve Estate, by Paul Rodrigues.*|||



[#image: /photos/65d44e14f3a9d1555ccb3647]|||*Pro-tip: Let the Chesterfield breathe—keep it off rugs with heavy patterns, and pair it with visually minimal pieces (such as the marble centre piece in this Chennai home by Shreya Krishnan Design Office) to offset its stateliness.*|||

The Chesterfield sofa has travelled centuries—from British drawing rooms to modern lofts—without losing its quiet command. Born in 18th-century England and once synonymous with men’s clubs and cigar smoke, it has since been redefined by designers who replaced excess with elegance. Today’s Chesterfields shed their ornate scrolls for slimmer arms, matte leather, or even linen and velvet, achieving heritage without heaviness. What makes it timeless is its architecture: deep tufting that adds texture, symmetry that calms the eye, and a sense of permanence rare in fast-turn design. It anchors contemporary spaces with [history](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/bombay-deco-is-the-ultimate-chronicle-of-mumbais-art-deco-history/){: target="_blank"}, bringing gravitas to the lightest rooms and restraint to the grandest ones.

*Also read: [Poliform's Owen collection blends couture finesse with modern Italian craftsmanship](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/poliforms-owen-collection-blends-couture-finesse-with-modern-italian-craftsmanship/){: target="_blank"}*

## The Built-In Sofa: Seating As Architecture


[#image: /photos/68c28ba871e6a8c79d088741]|||*Pro-tip: Layer fixed seating with loose cushions and textiles in varied tones; the contrast of softness against masonry keeps it human, not austere. You can also experiment with other couch style pairings to add variety, as seen in this Mumbai home by RAD co \+ lab.*|||



[#image: /photos/67a9c0a9a0d263d60280eda7]|||*Pro-tip: You can get creative with your seating. In this Mumbai home by STaND Design, the deck flooring of the balcony extends as seating into the living room, creating an enticing play of levels.*|||

The built-in sofa blurs the line between furniture and structure—part of the room, not merely placed in it. Emerging from Mediterranean and [Japanese](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/this-2000-square-foot-home-in-bandra-blends-indian-and-japanese-design-sensibilities-pill-design-studio/){: target="_blank"} traditions where space was both precious and poetic, it has become a hallmark of architectural minimalism today. Cast in lime plaster, stone, or tadelakt, it offers permanence in an age of the temporary. Its beauty lies in proportion and restraint: seamless planes that invite lounging without visual noise. Built-ins work best when continuity is key—extending from plinth to wall to window in a single gesture. They turn corners into contemplative nooks, steps into seating, and architecture into atmosphere.

## The Low-Slung Lounge: Grounding Grace


[#image: /photos/67cfe1a75f709ec2a12b9048]|||*Pro-tip: Orient the low-slung sofa towards windows or courtyards—the low height extends the landscape. The surrounding furniture should be low and the lighting diffused as well, to allow the sofa to fully express its horizontal emphasis, as seen in this Bengaluru home by Ashni Kumar.*|||

The low-slung sofa captures the easy elegance of spaces that breathe—homes where air, light, and pause carry as much weight as design. Drawing from Japanese tatami culture and India’s own tradition of floor seating, it sits closer to the ground, promoting a slower rhythm and visual calm. Its elongated proportions and deep cushions create an atmosphere of ease, while wide arms and open bases invite lounging that feels instinctive rather than staged. Designers love it for how it grounds a space—anchoring large [living rooms](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/9-living-room-decor-ideas-thatll-make-this-space-the-highlight-of-your-home/){: target="_blank"} without interrupting sightlines or scale.

In conclusion, across all silhouettes, the most timeless sofa isn’t defined by style but by spatial awareness and sensitivity—to proportion, texture, and light. Keep upholstery tactile, colour palettes grounded, and silhouettes human in scale. Let materials breathe, allow light to move, and balance every softness with structure. Above all, invest in craftsmanship and comfort; trends fade, but good design—like a well-made sofa—only gets better with time.

title: Mick Jagger ha un magnifico castello francese del XVIII secolo con vista sulla Valle della Loira
content:
**Mick Jagger ha un magnifico castello francese del XVIII secolo con vista sulla Valle della Loira.**

Nel dipartimento dell’Indre-et-Loire, nella regione del Centro della [Francia](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/castello-francia-foresta-chantilly-residenza-jeanne-rothschild/){: target="_blank"}, lo Château de Fourchette fa da cornice a Pocé-sur-Cisse. L’edificio settecentesco, ex dimora del duca Étienne-François de Choiseul (1719-1785), si erge orgoglioso circondato da circa venti ettari di [giardini](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/giardini-da-visitare-almeno-una-volta-nella-vita/){: target="_blank"} formali.



[#image: /photos/69181948d4dc5fb820563d76]|||La Fourchette, il castello seicentesco in Touraine del cantante dei Rolling Stones Mick Jagger, nell'ottobre 1983.|||

## Una proprietà enorme con lago, cascata e pagoda
Costruita a forma rettangolare con un primo piano e una [mansarda](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/piccola-mansarda-42-m2-madrid-atmosfere-cinematografiche-wes-anderson/){: target="_blank"}, la proprietà appartiene al **celebre rocker e musicista britannico** **Mick Jagger**. Ha acquistato il [castello](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/castello-california-san-diego-serie-televisiva-trono-spade/){: target="_blank"} nel 1980 per una cifra di 2,2 milioni di franchi e **lo visita regolarmente per festeggiare il suo compleanno ogni [estate](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/isole-in-italia-estate-turismo-architettura/){: target="_blank"}**, il 26 luglio. Secondo la leggenda, la sera stessa dallo Château de Fourchette vengono fatti esplodere dei fuochi d’artificio per celebrare l’occasione. Oltre al parco gigantesco, che comprende un laghetto, una cascata, una piscina e un campo [da tennis](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/da-dubai-a-monaco-i-10-campi-da-tennis-piu-belli-del-mondo/){: target="_blank"}, il [castello](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/castello-borgogna-francia-contemporaneo-trasformazione/){: target="_blank"} ha una pagoda giapponese nel giardino.



[#image: /photos/69181948d4dc5fb820563d77]|||PARIGI, FRANCIA - 1985: il musicista Mick Jagger, membro dei Rolling Stones, passeggia davanti al suo castello La Fouchette nel 1985.|||

## Nel castello di Fourchette c’è uno studio di registrazione
Qui i Rolling Stones avrebbero registrato l’album *A Bigger Bang* del 2005, il loro primo lavoro in studio dopo una pausa di otto anni. Lo Château de Fourchette ha infatti un proprio [studio di registrazione](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/brad-pitt-studio-di-registrazione-designer-damien-quintard/){: target="_blank"} professionale, dove il rocker a volte si filma su [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/p/B-2HP7uhwn_/?hl=fr){: target="_blank"}. La scelta di Mick Jagger per la Touraine non è stata casuale. Ha trascorso parte delle vacanze durante la sua infanzia proprio nella regione, nel campeggio Ile d’Or.

Durante il lockdown, Mick Jagger ha fatto molti video all’interno del castello, e questi filmati sono stati condivisi e e commentati dal **famoso conduttore americano Jimmy Fallon del Tonight Show**. I video mostrano il [cantante](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/patty-pravo-case-stile-cantante-italiana-eta/){: target="_blank"} impegnato in attività che svolge in campagna come la cura dell’orto e la potatura dei cespugli di rose. Si vede Mick Jagger ridipingere anche i cancelli della casa, suonare la chitarra in giardino, riparare il motore della sua auto, giocare con le sue pecore e cucinare.



[#image: /photos/69181948d4dc5fb820563d78]|||Jerry Hall allo Château de La Fourchette con Taritha, 1986. Jerry Hall ha appena rilasciato un'intervista a una rivista britannica *di celebrità*.|||

## L’artigiano dei tessuti che ha lavorato nel castello
La gente del posto lo conosce e partecipa alla sua vita quotidiana quando è nei paraggi. Il documentario *Château de Fourchette, le château de Mick Jagger*, trasmesso su France 3 Orléans nel 2011 e archiviato dall’[INA](https://www.ina.fr/ina-eclaire-actu/video/or00001446820/poce-sur-cisse-chateau-de-fourchette-le-chateau-de-mick-jagger){: target="_blank"}, racconta questi momenti. In particolare, il giornalista incontra il l’artigiano tappezziere che ha creato l’arredamento della residenza; al momento del servizio è in pensione, ma racconta i suoi ricordi. L’uomo che “ha lavorato sui preziosi tessuti di Fourchette” spiega che **i tessuti all’interno del [castello](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/castello-dal-pozzo-lago-maggiore-hotel-ristorante-vacanze/){: target="_blank"} catturano la luce**: “A seconda della luce, si ottiene il rosso o il giallo. A seconda del sole, la stanza cambia colore”. Il tappezziere prosegue: “È stata più di una semplice opportunità, perché ho potuto fare cose che altrimenti non avrei mai fatto. È l’unica volta che ho posato tessuti così belli. È come per alcune delle poltrone che ho realizzato: sinceramente non sapevo realizzarle prima”, confida.

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/69181948d4dc5fb820563d79]|||VIENNA, AUSTRIA - 1973: Mick Jagger posa in un castello nel settembre 1973 a Vienna, in Austria.|||

[#image: /photos/69181948d4dc5fb820563d75]|||Mick Jagger fotografato nel 1973 in un castello di Vienna, in Austria.|||

+++

Ricorda l’aneddoto di quando dovette rivestire i lavabi del castello di Fourchette: “Quando l’arredatore mi disse che dovevamo rivestire i lavabi, io gli dissi: ‘Come faccio?’” Poi presenta i preziosi tessuti provenienti dall’Inghilterra che adornano i [divani](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/arredamento-linving-divani-poltrone-imbottiti-milano-design-week-2025/){: target="_blank"} di Mick Jagger, dicendo che il mix è bello ed eclettico. “Non è appariscente, è di buon gusto, ci si sente come a casa, non come in un museo”, conclude. Un’opportunità per scoprire i tessuti sontuosi su cui l’artigiano ha lavorato per quindici giorni per perfezionare ogni minimo dettaglio. Sorride con modestia: “Per una poltrona realizzata per Mick Jagger ho chiesto lo stesso prezzo che per qualsiasi altro cliente”.

Articolo originale pubblicato su [*AD Francia*](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/mick-jagger-chateau-francais-vallee-loire){: target="_blank"}, adattato da Paola Corazza.

### Leggi anche:
- [Arredare con i mobili vintage: 7 case fantastiche a cui ispirarsi](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/arredare-mobili-vintage-idee-case-decorate-pezzi-online-mercatini-usato/)
- [Una casa sull’isola di Gotland, in Svezia, è il buen retiro di Malin Glemme, fondatrice del brand Layered, e dei suoi tre figli](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-isola-gotland-svezia-malin-glemme-arredamento-minimalista-stile-nordico/#intcid=_ad-it-right-rail_d615982a-1a3b-4fa3-8de0-3ca53e463fb9_popular4-2){: target="_blank"}
- [Lenny Kravitz vuole che a casa vostra “le cose respirino”](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/lenny-kravitz-vuole-che-a-casa-vostra-le-cose-respirino/){: target="_blank"}
