title: 13 Most Beautiful Islands in the World To Visit – and the Books To Read While You’re There
content:
**From the Maldives and Hawaii to Madagascar and Sir Bani Yas Island, discover the most beautiful islands in the world, places where rare wildlife, untamed landscapes, exceptional [hotels](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/best-hotels-in-the-world-2025-design-experts-share-their-favourite-stays){: target="_blank"} and pristine beaches converge to create visions of paradise.**

## 1\. Socotra, Yemen


[#image: /photos/699d910bf106a095cbec5304]

Floating in the Arabian Sea, closer to the Horn of Africa than [mainland Yemen](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/welcome-to-the-worlds-steepest-villages){: target="_blank"}, Socotra is often described as the most alien-looking island on Earth. Isolated for millions of years, it has evolved its own visual language: forests of umbrella-shaped Dragon’s Blood trees, limestone plateaus, and blindingly white dunes spilling into turquoise waters. Nearly a third of its plant life exists nowhere else, earning the archipelago UNESCO World Heritage status. Despite its otherworldly appearance, life on Socotra follows ancient rhythms, with small fishing villages, frankincense trees, and untouched beaches stretching for kilometres without interruption. It is not a place of resorts or spectacle, but of raw, elemental beauty – a reminder of what the natural world looked like before it was shaped by tourism.

**Our favourite hotel in Socotra:** Socotra Eco-Lodge, offering simple stays immersed in nature  
**Books to read while you're there:** _The Monk of Mokha_ by Dave Eggers – Follows a young Yemeni-American attempting to revive the Yemeni coffee industry amidst the escalating civil war, offering a portrait of resilience.  
[*welcometosocotra.com*](https://www.welcometosocotra.com/){: target="_blank"}

## 2\. Sir Bani Yas Island, United Arab Emirates


[#image: /photos/699d8f7af106a095cbec529e]

Off the coast of Abu Dhabi, Sir Bani Yas Island offers a rare proposition: a safari experience unfolding entirely on an island. Once the private nature reserve of Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, the founding father of the UAE, the island today protects over 17,000 free-roaming animals, from Arabian oryx and gazelles to giraffes roaming freely against a backdrop of rugged mountains and coastal plains. Covering nearly half the island, the Arabian Wildlife Park sits alongside secluded beaches, mangroves, and archaeological remains, including the ruins of a 7th-century Christian monastery, offering a glimpse into the region’s layered past. Visitors can explore by 4x4, kayak through calm coastal waters, or simply retreat into the stillness of its desert-meets-sea landscape. Remote yet accessible, Sir Bani Yas represents a vision of conservation, hospitality, and heritage in balance.

**Our favourite hotel in Sir Bani Yas Island:** Anantara Desert Islands Resort, set between savannah and sea  
**Books to read while you're there:** *The Ring of the Dove* by Ibn Hazm – A classic treatise on the nature of love and human relationships.  
[*sirbaniyas.ae*](https://www.sirbaniyas.ae/){: target="_blank"}

## 3\. Madagascar


[#image: /photos/699d97dbf106a095cbec5414]

The fourth-largest island in the world, Madagascar drifts off the southeast coast of Africa like a forgotten continent, its landscapes shaped by millions of years of isolation. This separation has given rise to extraordinary biodiversity found nowhere else on Earth: forests of towering baobab trees, limestone pinnacles known as tsingy, and rare wildlife such as lemurs, chameleons and fossas. Along its coastline, deserted beaches meet warm turquoise waters, while inland, rainforests, canyons and highlands reveal constantly shifting terrain. Both raw and poetic, Madagascar offers a rare sense of discovery.

**Our favourite hotel in Madagascar:** Miavana by Time \+ Tide, set on a private island off the northeast coast  
**Books to read while you're there:** *The Aye-Aye and I* by Gerald Durrell – Documenting Durell's last expedition to this mystical country, The Aye-Aye treks the reader through Madagascar's glorious wildlife and the animals on the verge of extinction.    
[*madagascar-tourisme.com*](https://madagascar-tourisme.com/en/){: target="_blank"}

## 4\. Barbados
In the heart of the Caribbean, this coral island – the only one in the archipelago – blends exotic flavours with wild coastlines and 100 kilometres of beaches (over 80 beaches, all public). On land, you'll discover nature reserves – and its national mascot, the Green Verbena, a mischievous little monkey – sugar cane fields, coconut groves, refreshing waterfalls, as well as golf, polo and cricket, a direct legacy of the Commonwealth, from which the island gained independence in 2021. On the seaside, the wind conditions are perfect for surfing and kitesurfing, the reefs for diving, sea caves specific to its geology, beaches for watching turtles lay their eggs between April and November, and the flying fish offshore. In terms of typical Caribbean architecture, the sublime colonial residences in the [UNESCO-listed](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/new-unesco-world-heritage-site-southern-ohio-hopewell-ceremonial-earthworks){: target="_blank"} historic centre of the capital, Bridgetown, are a must-see, as are the famous coral stone villas along the coast designed by British theatre designer Oliver Messel and now a national heritage site.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def55]

**Our favourite hotel in Barbados**: the recently renovated Fairmont Royal Pavilion  
**Books to read while you're there**: *Even in paradise* by Elizabeth Nunez – An epic tale of family betrayal and manipulation couched in terrifically engaging prose and peopled with sketchy characters.  
[*visitbarbados.org*](http://visitbarbados.org/){: target="_blank"}

## 5\. Jamaica
Jamaica is the second Caribbean destination on AD's list of the most beautiful islands in the world, and for good reason. The island's north coast boasts some of the most stunning hidden beaches, including Frenchman's Cove – the legendary beach featured in the 1988 film *Cocktail* starring [Tom Cruise](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/where-does-tom-cruise-live-inside-the-mission-impossible-stars-real-estate-portfolio){: target="_blank"} – and *Seven Mile Beach*. Fine sand, turquoise water, palm trees: all the ingredients are there. But every stop between Montego Bay and Port Antonio is a delight to discover. Stroll through the spicy, multicoloured nugget markets, the local plantations, the fried, smoked or barbecued specialities on the street corner, always to the sound of music... Get off the beaten track and opt for a zip-line through the canopy, a dive in the Blue Lagoon (it was the setting for the famous Brooke Shields movie), visit a historic rum factory and its vertiginous stills in the middle of the jungle, and dip into its [heavenly waterfalls](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/most-spectacular-waterfalls-from-around-the-world){: target="_blank"}. Climb to an altitude of 2,000 meters to sip a cup of coffee at Blue Mountain, one of the world's finest, whose estate is a UNESCO World Heritage site. Inland, take a pirogue along the Rio Grande, and a little further on, take a break at the Goldeneye Hotel, where Ian Fleming wrote his first novel with an emblematic character: [James Bond](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/james-bond-villains-moroccan-villa-from-spectre-is-for-sale-for-dollar29-million){: target="_blank"}.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def56]

**Our favourite hotel in Jamaica**: Geejam, with its jungle huts  
**Books to read while you're there:** _The Lunatic_ by Anthony Winkler – A humorous and poignant look at life in rural Jamaica.  
[*fr.visitjamaica.com*](http://fr.visitjamaica.com/){: target="_blank"}

## 6\. Corsica
The wildest of the [Mediterranean islands](https://www.admiddleeast.com/gallery/modern-and-minimal-holiday-home-in-menorca-spain){: target="_blank"} boasts an astonishing diversity of landscapes, from north to south, along its almost 1,000 km of unspoilt coastline. From the pink granite rocks of the Calanques de Piana to the immaculate limestone cliffs of Bonifacio and the [black sandy beach](https://www.admiddleeast.com/gallery/9-beautiful-beaches-around-the-world-that-are-just-as-bizarre-as-they-are-stunning){: target="_blank"} of Nonza, the landscapes are majestic and never the same. Just as pleasant is the inland, bursting with freshwater, torrents, rivers and lakes to cool off in summer. Discover the hot springs at Caldane, Taccana or Baracci – which is due to reopen with its historic hotel. With 240 days of sunshine a year, the beaches of the Isle of Beauty are consistently ranked among the best in the world. In summer, when the island is saturated, people seek absolute peace in its typical villages – a local tradition while travellers enjoy the coastline – in search of shade and traditional inns, like Chez Séraphin, in Péri. With the refurbishment of the Domaine du Mouflon d'Or in Zonza, at the foot of the breathtaking Aiguilles de Bavella, Corsica's mountain side is slowly coming back to life... In the meantime, its popular beach clubs, private villas and ecolodges continue to be the stuff of dreams.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def57]

**Our favourite hotel in Corsica**: Le Domaine de Murtoli, in the south of the island  
**Books to read while you're there**: *The Amalfi Secret* by Dean & Catherine Reineking – A fast-paced mystery thriller featuring a family vacation turned into a dangerous hunt for secrets across the Amalfi Coast and Rome.  
[*visit-corsica.com*](http://www.visit-corsica.com/){: target="_blank"}



[#externallink: /externallinks/65030450bb917946d989f4c2]

## 7\. The Seychelles
The Indian Ocean boasts many of the most beautiful islands in the world, listed here, but the Seychelles are truly special: Off the coast of East Africa, between Madagascar and Reunion Island, the Seychelles is an A-list island paradise unlike any other. A stopover on the Spice and Silk Routes, the archipelago was discovered by Vasco da Gama in 1503. With no fewer than 115 granite and coral islands – spread over more than 1,000 km – the Seychelles boast some of the most beautiful beaches in the world: Praslin, Anse Georgette, North Island, La Digue... Distinguishable from the rest by their polished rocks that seem to have fallen randomly from the sky onto white sandy beaches, the Seychelles are the ultimate version of paradise on Earth. You can easily island hop, discover giant tortoises, dive with sharks for the ultimate thrill and enjoy water sports. On land, you can discover mangroves, waterfalls and rare animals such as tiger chameleons and black parrots, between two sessions of golf or climbing on the famous flat rocks. Then it's time to *relax*. After all, it's 29°C all year round… and the island is protected from cyclones.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def58]

**Our favourite hotel in The Seychelles**: [Cheval Blanc](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/the-cheval-blanc-seychelles-what-its-like-to-stay-at-lvmhs-lush-indian-ocean-getaway){: target="_blank"}, the latest addition to the prestigious collection designed by architect Jean-Michel Gathy  
**Books to read while you're there**: *A Psalm for the Wild-Built* by Becky Chambers– A story about a tea monk (like a traveling therapist who serves tea) who meets a robot in the wilderness, and they converse about the differences in human and robot society and what it means to have a life's purpose.  
[*seychelles.fr*](http://www.seychelles.fr/){: target="_blank"}

## 8\. Mauritius
A gentle lifestyle and a tropical rhythm: Mauritius is an exotic, historic destination whose excellent service is an absolute benchmark in the world of hospitality. In the Indian Ocean, 800km east of Madagascar, Mauritius boasts picture-perfect beaches, often flanked by a panorama of the vertiginous Morne Brabant mountain in the south-west, with an average annual temperature of 25°C and 280 days of sunshine a year. On the agenda: golf, hiking, water sports, basta. Neither a show-off nor exhausting, the island has nothing left to prove about its paradisiacal appeal, which it deploys with grace and consistency. Its ultra-lush landscape allows you to discover grandiose ancestral forests and fascinating fauna. One of the most unforgettable experiences is to cross the Black River National Park, the largest national park and a veritable [sanctuary of biodiversity](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/beautiful-sustainable-buildings-green-architecture-thats-healing-our-planet){: target="_blank"}. Botany reigns supreme here, as in the Pamplemousse gardens, a legacy of the island's colonial past. What to pack: vanilla, rum, spices and black tea.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def59]

**Our favourite hotel in Mauritius**: The Royal Palm Beachcomber Luxury, an island institution  
**Books to read while you're there**: *The White Tiger* by Aravind Adiga (2008) A Booker Prize-winning novel that uses the term "white tiger" metaphorically to represent a unique individual in Indian society.  
[*mauritiusnow.com*](http://www.mauritiusnow.com/){: target="_blank"}

## 9\. Hawaii
With 137 [volcanic](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/how-cappadocia-turkeys-underground-cave-city-was-built-design-lovers-travel-bucket-list){: target="_blank"} islands, the Hawaiian archipelago is the only US state outside the North American continent. In the heart of Oceania, a 6-hour flight from Los Angeles, it's an unforgettable destination for thrill-seekers and Zen-cravers alike. Steep cliffs, sometimes crashing waves, the scent of tiare, black sandy beaches, and so many climates that accommodate a super-powerful natural environment, from tropical forests to lunar beaches. People come here to get married, facing the ocean and celebrate weddings, anniversaries, rebirths, in short, an exceptional moment. They bring back pink Hawaiian salt, flowery shirts, pearly shells and unforgettable souvenirs. In winter, you can watch humpback whales and soak up the diversity of this legendary archipelago.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def5a]

**Our favourite hotel in Hawaii**: Four Seasons Lanai, totally exclusive  
**Books to read while you're there**: *Hula* by Jasmine Iolani Hakes – A novel set in Hilo in the 1970s that delves into the culture and history of hula.  
[*gohawaii.com*](https://www.gohawaii.com/){: target="_blank"}

## 10\. The Maldives
With 1,200 islands in its archipelago, spread over almost 800 km, the [Maldives](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/i-stayed-at-soneva-secret-in-the-maldives-heres-what-its-really-like){: target="_blank"} are a real head-turner. Of course, each island, almost privatised by a hotel brand – and all the big names are there – is an experience in itself, with villas on stilts, on the beach, with private swimming pools, a cinema with your feet in the sand and dinner for two on a tiny desert island. The Maldives are the perfect destination for honeymooners and, on some islands, families. On the postcard, an atoll, coral galore, crystal-clear water, face-to-face with manta rays, baby sharks and all manner of unknown exotic beasts, sushi dinners and fresh papaya for breakfast, a phantasmagorical spa with a view, total relaxation in front of sparkling lagoons. Irresistible. The Maldives is the terrestrial incarnation of a tropical paradise, which is developing ever more sustainable experiences to protect its flora and fauna: restoration of the coral reef, protection of sea turtles, meetings with marine biologists, etc.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def5b]

**Our favourite hotel in The Maldives**: One & Only Reethi Ra  
**Books to read while you're there**: *The Soul of an Octopus* by Sy Montgomery – A Surprising Exploration into the Wonder of Consciousness.  
[*visitmaldives.com*](https://visitmaldives.com/en){: target="_blank"}

## 11\. Zanzibar
Nicknamed the Spice Island, just 35 km east of the African coast, Zanzibar is a paradise-like interlude. Made up of three main islands (Unguja, Pemba, Mafia), a former sultanate and then British colony, the archipelago boasts an equatorial climate, influences from Africa, the [Arabian Peninsula](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/inspired-by-moroccan-palaces-this-home-in-mangaluru-india-reframes-islamic-art-traditions-for-a-new-generation){: target="_blank"} and [India](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/this-sprawling-safari-lodge-in-india-aramness-is-a-modern-day-sanctuary){: target="_blank"}, and is the cradle of Swahili culture. A kite spot and a national forest, the archipelago is a lush sanctuary inhabited by free-living red monkeys and blue vervet monkeys. A UNESCO World Heritage Site, its capital – the birthplace of Freddy Mercury – is a city steeped in history. Stroll through the spice market, its narrow streets with carved doors and its old fort. Discover the mangroves, the seaweed aquaculture traditionally managed by the women, the lagoons with their thousand shades of turquoise, everything has an exotic flavour in Zanzibar.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def53]

**Our favourite hotel in Zanzibar**: White Sand, Tanzania's only Relais & Châteaux  
**Books to read while you're there**: *The Famished Road* by Ben Okri – Known for his magical realism, Okri (of Nigerian descent) became the youngest winner of the Booker Prize in 1991 for the novel.   
[*zanzibar.com*](https://www.zanzibar.com/){: target="_blank"}

## 12\. Tuamotu, French Polynesia
If there's a paradise far away from it all, it's French Polynesia. The 76 Tuamotu atolls are part of this timeless archipelago, 4,000 km – and many hours on a plane – from land. A journey well worth making... We could draw with our eyes closed its idyllic scenery, like those once painted by Gauguin. Coral islands stretching for almost 2000 km, the Tuamotu (*"distant islands"* in Tahitian) are famous for their treasures, exported to the other side of the world, such as *monoi* – made from tiare flowers and coconut oil – fresh fish, salt and the region's treasure, Tahitian black pearls. With your feet in the sand or in the crystal-clear water, levitating in [a villa on stilts](https://www.admiddleeast.com/gallery/incredible-overwater-bungalow-resorts-to-book-around-the-world){: target="_blank"} or on a boat, but always far from the crowds, this is island living at its best in the heart of the Pacific Ocean. Absolute rest and total disconnection are almost mandatory in the face of these insolently beautiful lagoons and unreal coral rings.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def5c]

**Our favourite hotel in Tuamotu, French Polynesia**: The newly renovated Tikehau by Pearl Resorts  
**Books to read while you're there**: *Entangled Life* by Merlin Sheldrake – A mystical, scientific search into mycorrhizal fungi, which form vast, interconnected networks in the soil, linking plants and trees to transfer nutrients and information, often referred to as the "wood wide web".  
[*tahititourisme.fr*](https://www.tahititourisme.fr/){: target="_blank"}

## 13\. Saint-Barthélemy
Rounding up our list of the most beautiful islands in the world is a tiny Caribbean island (25 square-kilometres) that isn't exactly deserted anymore, except when you find yourself at dawn on one of its sublime beaches (Saint-Jean, Lorient, Gouverneur, Colombier, etc.). Saint-Barthélémy is home to 65 restaurants and 12 hotels, sublime, uncluttered residences with infinity pools, and boutiques by the biggest luxury brands. The clientele is mainly American, followed by the French. This island, discovered by Christopher Columbus and formerly Swedish, is a dream come true for its sumptuous end-of-year festivities, the highlight of its summer season, welcoming a flurry of private jets and [XXL yachts](https://www.admiddleeast.com/story/discover-7-superyachts-designed-by-famous-architects){: target="_blank"} to its marina in Gustavia. On the beach, there's surfing at Toiny, hiking trails and the natural pools of Grand Fond, but above all, the national sport of *idleness*, cocktail in hand. St. Barth has become the quintessential *socialite* island paradise, where people come to see and be seen. Beyond its bling lifestyle, Saint-Barth remains a mysterious island in the French West Indies that continues to fascinate, and rightly so, with its legends and pirates, ancient... and contemporary.



[#image: /photos/6842ff022201b76e6d4def5d]

**Our favourite hotel in Saint-Barthélemy**: L'Eden Rock, with its feet in the water  
**Books to read while you're there**: *The Adventures of Amina al-Sirafi* by Shannon Chakraborty – A modern tale of a retired pirate captain returning for one last adventure.  
[*saintbarth-tourisme.com*](http://www.saintbarth-tourisme.com/){: target="_blank"}



[#externallink: /externallinks/64e5df0fa0b7b46ccab99ad6]

title: A Mosca, questo appartamento contemporaneo svela un fascino senza tempo e viste spettacolari
content:
**A Mosca, questo appartamento contemporaneo svela un fascino senza tempo e viste spettacolari.**

Cercare la bellezza e renderla tangibile: è il filo conduttore che ha guidato l’interior designer **Olesya Gelman-Dobrovolskaya** nella progettazione della sua casa di famiglia. Situato a [Mosca,](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/appartamento-mosca-estetica-francese-oggetti-moderni-mobili-su-misura/) l’appartamento contemporaneo di 159 metri quadri, al nono piano di un edificio moderno, si apre sulle vedute urbane di Kutuzovsky Prospekt e della Presnenskaya Embankment. «L’ispirazione è arrivata dallo storico Ukraine Hotel, che si staglia poco lontano. Le sue colonne di cristallo e il parquet francese a spina di pesce hanno dettato il tono: desideravo un interno che evocasse la raffinatezza dell’[Art Déco](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-art-deco-tocco-contemporaneo/), ma con un’anima calda, vissuta, personale», racconta Gelman-Dobrovolskaya. «L’idea alla base del progetto era quella di realizzare un interno che incorniciasse e abbracciasse la bellezza della città. Oltre a creare una disposizione funzionale e adatta alla mia famiglia, volevo creare uno spazio arricchito da [opere d’arte](https://www.ad-italia.it/gallery/stupefacenti-trompe-loeil-in-tutto-il-mondo-italia/) e arredi di design, pezzi d’autore che raccontano una storia e hanno un valore sentimentale», prosegue la designer.



[#image: /photos/684be3fdf9532a44b9c9c21b]|||Uno scorcio del soggiorno. Arredi: divano di Pouenat Humbert and Poyet; poltrone di Gallotti&Radice, design Dainelli Studio; tavolini di Alma de Luce.|||

## Spazi fluidi e luminosi
L’appartamento si estende su tre facciate, inondate di luce naturale grazie ad ampie porte-finestre, balconi alla Giulietta e soffitti alti che raggiungono i 3,5 metri in tutti gli ambienti principali. «Abbiamo mantenuto l’altezza originale ovunque, tranne che nei bagni e nella lavanderia, dove è stato integrato il sistema di ventilazione. Le porte, alte 2,4 metri - alcune a doppio battente - amplificano la monumentalità dello spazio», spiega l’interior designer.



[#image: /photos/684be3fff9532a44b9c9c21d]|||Un angolo del living: chandelier Visual Comfort, Julie Neill; opera d'arte “I” di Svetlana Chimitdorzhina, Galleria RELOFT; credenza John Richard; piatto in pietra vintage Maitland Smith.|||

La planimetria è articolata attorno a un ingresso generoso, che distribuisce il flusso tra soggiorno, sala da pranzo e cucina. Quest’ultima, dotata di un doppio accesso - dal corridoio e dalla zona giorno - favorisce una fruizione fluida e circolare degli spazi. La zona notte ospita una suite padronale con bagno en suite e cabina armadio, oltre a una suite indipendente per la figlia, completa di camera, bagno e spogliatoio.



[#image: /photos/684be3fc8b37e4d11a14aee7]|||Living con vista panoramica. Lampada da terra Holly Hunt; divano di Pouenat Humbert and Poyet; tavolini Alma de Luce.|||

A guidare le scelte progettuali hanno contribuito la luce naturale e la vista mozzafiato. «Ho scelto una palette cromatica neutra, ispirata alla natura. Toni sobri ed eleganti, accenti in bronzo, superfici in marmo e dettagli in foglia d’oro applicata a mano raccontano la raffinatezza del progetto», dice Dobrovolskaya. All’ingresso, il soggiorno accoglie con arredi firmati dalla maison francese Pouenat 1880 e dal brand americano Holly Hunt, mentre la cucina di Molteni&Co - disegnata da Vincent Van Duysen - si distingue per il piano in marmo scultoreo, che la trasforma nel cuore pulsante della casa, perfetta per cene intime e momenti conviviali.

## Cromie delicate infondono serenità
Toni delicati e texture raffinate definiscono così l’estetica degli interni: una base neutra su cui si innestano finiture tessili, inserti preziosi e materiali tattili. L’illuminazione, curata da L1 Group, introduce scenari personalizzabili, pensati per adattarsi al ritmo della giornata. Nel soggiorno, nicchie incassate ospitano ceramiche d’epoca, vasi e libri, mentre la parete TV è impreziosita da una superficie in foglia d’oro che richiama leggere scaglie sospese. I tessuti giocano anche un ruolo fondamentale nell’atmosfera dell’appartamento. «Ho scelto la seta per il soggiorno, la master bedroom e la cabina armadio; mentre il cotone per la stanza di mia figlia, più calda e accogliente. Nei bagni e nel corridoio, pannelli murali in XPE aggiungono un tocco tattile e sofisticato, trasformando gli ambienti in veri rifugi gioiello», aggiunge la designer.



[#image: /photos/684be4038b37e4d11a14aeeb]|||L'angolo lettura nella camera da letto della figlia. Scrivania Aspen di Tosconova, poltrona Queen Anne di Baker Furniture, lampada da tavolo Austen Large di Visual Comfort e opere d'arte di Irina Davis.|||

## L’arte come elemento distintivo
Non mancano opere d’arte selezionate e distribuite con precisione curatoriale. Nel soggiorno, il dipinto *“I”* di Svetlana Chimitdorzhina – ex allieva di Aidan Salakhova – dialoga con un piatto in pietra vintage di Maitland Smith (Chahan Gallery), portato da Parigi. Un pannello di Lev Efimov cattura l’attenzione, mentre le ceramiche di Maria Toropygina e Victoria Dego completano le nicchie espositive. All’ingresso campeggia un’opera di Ksenia Berestova, mentre nella master suite trova spazio la scultura in bronzo *“Thinking Woman”* di Aude Herlédan (Galerie d’Art Paris 1831), accanto a un pannello in seta di François Mascarello (Galerie BSL). Un luogo che celebra il dettaglio, sospeso tra materia, luce e memoria.



[#image: /photos/684be3fd8a22cf840d37345e]|||Un angolo della camera della figlia. Specchio Uttermost; letto Ligne Roset.|||



[#image: /photos/684be3fd8a22cf840d37345f]|||La camera padronale. Scultura Lev Efimov; letto Sofa & Chair; applique da parete Holly Hunt Paul Mathieu; lampadario Visual Comfort.|||

title: Yucca plants in India: A desert native finds its place indoors
content:
Spiky yet [sculptural](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/in-this-chennai-penthouse-spanning-5100-square-feet-luxury-lives-in-the-quietest-details/){: target="_blank"}, yucca plants have emerged as a plant of choice for those looking for more design forward houseplants. Long valued in the West for its resilience, it is now taking root in Indian homes where simplicity and strong design language are increasingly favoured.

Yuccas may hail from the deserts of North and Central America, but their drought-hardy nature makes them ideal candidates for [Indian climates](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/this-mumbai-office-adapts-japandi-design-to-the-indian-context/){: target="_blank"}. They adapt well to heat and inconsistent watering. Unlike more temperamental tropical houseplants, they require minimal attention and thrive in lean, well-drained soil.

## Design Appeal
Yuccas have a clean, upright shape that brings structure to any room. Their strong lines and sword-like leaves contrast nicely with softer, [trailing plants](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/10-low-maintenance-hanging-plants-that-will-enliven-your-home-while-saving-space/){: target="_blank"}. This makes them a useful choice when arranging greenery in modern interiors. They work particularly well in homes with minimalist or industrial features, with materials like concrete, metal, and wood.

[Outdoors](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/inside-a-calming-thrissur-home-where-the-outdoors-inspire-the-indoors/){: target="_blank"}, it functions as a natural focal point. Yuccas offer a crisp, upright silhouette that gives structure to both rooms and plant displays. Their strong, spiky leaves set them apart from the looser shapes of tropical or trailing species, making them ideal as a visual anchor. They bring order to indoor plant groupings and add rhythm to otherwise soft schemes.

## Energy and Symbolism
From a spiritual and Vastu point of view, yuccas embody active, upward energy. Their pointed leaves and vertical stance are thought to stimulate [stagnant zones](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/4-feng-shui-secrets-to-attract-money-and-abundance/){: target="_blank"}, particularly in north-facing spaces where energy tends to settle. Though not traditionally part of Indian spiritual or botanical customs, yuccas can still serve as tools for intentional space design.

Their presence signifies quiet strength and self-sufficiency, traits increasingly valued in urban home environments where simplicity is an aesthetic virtue.

## Types of Yuccas Available in India
Several species and cultivars of yucca plants are now common in [Indian nurseries](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/6-bedroom-plants-that-can-help-you-sleep-better/){: target="_blank"} and online platforms, selected for their ornamental appeal and low maintenance nature.

title: This weekend home in Kerala listens with intent to its lush valley setting
content:
For Anish Antony, founder of [Kerala](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/this-kerala-home-is-a-fusion-of-tropical-modernism-and-contemporary-details/){: target="_blank"}\-based Rare Earth Architects, this weekend home in Kerala served as a powerful articulation of the design philosophies he holds close to his heart: contextual sensitivity, [sustainability](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/in-raipur-a-clubhouse-gets-the-details-right-even-as-it-has-an-eye-on-sustainability/){: target="_blank"} and human-scaled architecture. “When designing, we listen, deeply, to both land and client, and translate our understanding into spaces that are timeless, functional and emotionally relevant,” he begins. This 2,250-square-foot home—situated on a one-acre site in the lush Pamba valley—was to replace an existing structure and serve as a restful and nurturing environment for the homeowner’s parents as well as a weekend retreat for him and his partner. “The home held sentimental value, and the owner emphasised his hope for an architecture of comfort without excess, and a built form that would feel like a natural extension of the landscape they all loved,” Antony explains.



[#image: /photos/6851824a147d851588211c0a]

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/685183328674b3e9dfcb9445]

[#image: /photos/685185202acfdc7781ae6306]

+++

An inside-out experiential narrative drives the story. The land became the inspiration—the slopes, the vegetation, the views—with the intention of letting the house emerge from the site, rather than impose upon it. An existing tree became a natural anchor. “It offered both shade and metaphorical grounding,” Antony smiles. To prioritize [spatial](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/this-south-mumbai-apartment-is-a-slice-of-french-spatial-chic-noorein-kapoor-designs-talib-chitawala-sam-wadekar/){: target="_blank"} openness, they followed an L-shaped plan, which encloses a semi-open yard to carve out a shaded microclimate. The living and dining areas are positioned centrally, spilling into a verandah that overlooks the best views of the valley.



[#image: /photos/685263ea6862f99ff64cc9c0]

Strategically relegated to the western and south-western edges, the [bedrooms](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/dhvani-bhanushali-to-alaya-f-chic-white-celebrity-bedrooms-to-add-to-your-moodboard/){: target="_blank"} leverage natural contours for both privacy and views. In fact, the balcony of the master bedroom, which fully opens up into the valley below, marks a defining favourite gesture for designer and owner alike. “It epitomises the home’s emotional and architectural intent, completely blurring the threshold between the inside and outside to offer a moment of immersive stillness,” explains Antony.



[#image: /photos/685186344b715364dc030073]

Sustainability is embedded into the design from the ground up, including retaining the site’s original slopes to reduce cut-and-fill. Brick masonry forms the bones of this architecture, while a custom-designed steel truss supports the [terracotta](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/5-cool-terracotta-homes-that-champion-vernacular-design-sensibilities/){: target="_blank"} tile roofing, enabling passive design strategies such as cross ventilation and heat escape. The plot’s 9-meter drop from the access road proved to be significantly challenging for the logistics of construction access and material delivery.



[#image: /photos/6851876696585e7daa207912]

Additionally, the structure’s southern and western faces undergo intense sun exposure, leading to heat-minimising architectural interventions such as recessed openings, shaded verandahs and optimised roof slopes. The functional addition of the western block also serves as a thermal buffer, minimising exposure to the sun. “For me, the design aligns with the tenets of tropical modernism—balancing contemporary aesthetics with climate-sensitive planning and contextual harmony,” Antony reveals.

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/6851869c4b715364dc030075]

[#image: /photos/685186b896585e7daa207910]

+++



[#image: /photos/685186ce4b715364dc030077]

Inside, a calm, meditative ambiance dominates as the [palette](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/a-gym-in-mumbais-amravati-is-really-a-mindful-escape-with-an-earthy-palette-architect-piyush-chandwani/){: target="_blank"} works towards the goal of visual quietness. Antony leans towards subtle, earth-toned finishes and materials specifically chosen for their ability to recede into the landscape. “I wanted to draw attention to the views, the textures, and the light rather than the interiors themselves,” he says. With storage integrated seamlessly into the walls, he paints a clean, uncluttered aesthetic that recedes into the background while allowing nature to play protagonist.

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/68518c1496585e7daa207918]

[#image: /photos/68518c1e96585e7daa20791a]

+++

Despite the homeowner’s deep involvement with the project throughout planning and construction—a fact that made this project truly collaborative in Antony’s eyes, allowing him to create a space that felt deeply personal—they were still surprised by some [elements](https://www.architecturaldigest.in/story/this-35000-square-foot-home-in-bengaluru-tastefully-incorporates-black-elements-roshni-ramnane/){: target="_blank"}, particularly how the passive design strategies created complete thermal comfort without any need for mechanical means.

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/6851885096585e7daa207916]

[#image: /photos/68518859e4b8b88f5887649e]

+++

From the form and orientation of this weekend home in Kerala to the materiality and even circulation paths, it listens, responds and integrates itself into the contours, climate and vegetation. “For me, this home isn’t just in the valley, it is part of it. It embodies a lifestyle that is mindful, grounded, and attuned to the rhythms of the valley,” concludes Antony.



[#cneembed: script/video/6458dba945afb67343abddb8.js]

title: Una casa di campagna radical chic immersa nella foresta fuori Istanbul: lo studio Sanayi313 sperimenta con forme semplici, dettagli audaci e un giardino ispirato a Kyoto
content:
## In questa casa di campagna radical chic firmata dallo studio turco Sanayi313 il minimalismo incontra il calore dell’abitare


[#image: /photos/6852cbd84277fc4ee544cf31]

A mezz’ora da **Istanbul**, immersa nella quiete della foresta di Belgrado, una casa in legno affacciata sul lago ripensa il concetto di [casa di campagna](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-campagna-toscana-argentari-alizee-brion-materiali-naturali/) in chiave radical chic. La struttura in legno su tre livelli, costruita in tronchi chiari, sembra emergere dalla natura anziché imporsi su di essa. Gli interni, affidati allo studio [**Sanayi313,**](https://sanayi313.com/en/){: target="_blank"} con sede a Istanbul, riflettono la stessa sobrietà misurata: [volumi calibrati, materiali autentici](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/come-ristrutturare-casa-campagna-senza-esperienza-my-tiny-estate/), colori neutri. Nessuna distrazione, solo essenziale. «L’orientamento, i materiali e la palette cromatica della casa prendono ispirazione direttamente dal paesaggio circostante. I tronchi che costituiscono la struttura principale sono stati dipinti in una tonalità beige chiaro e accostati a materiali e tessuti naturali, per aggiungere morbidezza agli interni», spiega **Enis Karavil, fondatore e direttore creativo di Sanayi313**.



[#image: /photos/6852cec976e65be6f3b6be21]

Il progetto nasce dal desiderio di una giovane coppia di costruire un rifugio intimo, [dove abitare con calma](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-di-campagna-rinnovata-coppia-creativa/), senza fretta. «L’obiettivo principale era progettare una casa rilassante, ma al tempo stesso dinamica e moderna per la giovane coppia», continua Karavil. Tutto ruota attorno alla relazione con l’esterno. La palette è neutra, i materiali grezzi: tessuti, pietra, legno, cuoio.



[#image: /photos/6852d3a63f0ae41d9048eca7]

**Elemento centrale del progetto è la scala**, completamente ridisegnata come fulcro architettonico e linea di [continuità tra i tre livelli della casa](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-campagna-inghilterra-piscina-sala-giochi-galleria-arte-studio-louise-holt-design/), un gesto fluido ma deciso, che allegerisce la struttura. «La sua curvatura morbida crea, a mio avviso, un bellissimo contrasto con l’architettura più spigolosa della casa. Collega con naturalezza piano principale, seminterrato e piano superiore, aggiungendo eleganza strutturale e un movimento organico alla rigidità dell’insieme», osserva il progettista.

## Un giardino come una sequenza di spazi


[#image: /photos/6852ce193f0ae41d9048ec9d]

Uno degli elementi più caratterizzanti della proprietà è il giardino terrazzato, pensato come un’[estensione del vivere domestico all’aperto.](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-campagna-copake-lake-design-nordico-quiete-upstate-new-york/) Affacciato sul lago, si svela come una sequenza di ambienti fluidi, progettati con cura per offrire momenti di relax e occasioni conviviali. «Una delle caratteristiche più distintive della casa è proprio **il giardino su più livelli,** progettato per rispondere sia al desiderio di tranquillità che per accogliere amici e condivere», spiega Karavil. La terrazza principale, con accesso diretto dal soggiorno, ospita una composizione della serie Borea di B&B Italia, divano tre posti e due poltrone, in contrasto cromatico con la facciata in legno scuro.



[#image: /photos/6852d10f3f0ae41d9048eca3]

«Durante la progettazione, abbiamo osservato attentamente lo stile di vita all’aperto della famiglia e assegnato a ciascun livello del giardino una funzione precisa, come il pontile costruito sul lago, l’area in ghiaia ispirata ai giardini di Kyoto, la zona pranzo e la terrazza panoramica che domina il paesaggio dall’alto», prosegue Karavil. Più in basso, al livello dell’acqua, si apre il giardino di ispirazione giapponese, in ghiaia, quasi del tutto vuoto, animato solo da una coppia di sedute Brutus di 101 Copenhagen. Ancora oltre, un pontile privato affacciato sul lago ospita due sedie Adirondack e la lampada da esterni Jaima di Joan Gaspar per Marset. Tutto è studiato per favorire la quiete, la contemplazione, la relazione con il paesaggio.

## Un soggiorno affacciato sul lago


[#image: /photos/6852d37376e65be6f3b6be25]

Il soggiorno si apre verso l’esterno con un layout asimmetrico e un’atmosfera rilassata, pensata per amplificare la vista sul paesaggio. Gli arredi dialogano tra volumi scultorei e linee semplici, in un linguaggio che unisce rigore e morbidezza. «Ho scelto un linguaggio di arredo fatto di forme scultoree, ma semplici», spiega Karavil. Su una delle pareti, un camino moderno a incasso con finitura in acciaio inox è integrato in una lunga panca in cemento. Rivestita da cuscini su entrambi i lati attraversa lo spazio come un segno architettonico deciso. Il resto dell’ambiente è costruito attorno a una selezione mirata di pezzi anni ’70: il sistema modulare Camaleonda disegnato da Mario Bellini, il divano Saint-Germain di Poliform, la Wiggle Chair di Frank Gehry e una coppia di tavolini 30313 firmati dallo studio Sanayi313. Il risultato è un ambiente ampio, rilassante e calibrato, in cui il paesaggio resta protagonista.

## Luce, misura e funzionalità


[#image: /photos/6852d3813f0ae41d9048eca5]

L’area pranzo, incastonata in una nicchia architettonica accanto al soggiorno, è essenziale e ben calibrata. Il tavolo 21313 disegnato da Sanayi313 dialoga con le sedute 0414 di Gallotti & Radice, mentre la sospensione Nuvola di Baxter introduce una tensione visiva morbida, senza forzature. La cucina guarda direttamente sul giardino ed è il cuore operativo della casa: semplice, funzionale, inondata di luce naturale. Accanto all’isola su misura in rovere, gli sgabelli Hven disegnati da Anton Björsing sottolineano il carattere pratico e moderno dello spazio. «La luce è sempre il punto di partenza di ogni progetto. L’orientamento dell’alba e del tramonto ha avuto un ruolo fondamentale nella definizione della casa. Ho voluto creare atmosfere diverse in ogni ambiente. Alla luce naturale abbiamo affiancato lampade mobili e paralumi, per ottenere un’illuminazione generale e al tempo stesso morbida e avvolgente», precisa Karavil.

## Sotto il tetto, la calma


[#image: /photos/6852d4383f0ae41d9048eca9]

Al piano superiore si trovano lo studio, nascosto in una nicchia con scrivania francese, LC7 e sgabello Tubular di Cassina, e la camera principale. Qui il soffitto spiovente e una grande finestra creano un’atmosfera raccolta. Il letto è centrato sotto la falda più alta. Il bagno accosta legno e marmo con un’estetica sobria e coerente.

## Una questione di equilibrio


[#image: /photos/6852d45976e65be6f3b6be27]

Tra sottrazione e dettaglio, rigore e comfort, questa casa di campagna riflette un’idea di abitare misurata, profondamente personale. Ogni scelta, dai materiali alle proporzioni, dalla luce ai volumi, è pensata per instaurare un dialogo diretto con il paesaggio e accompagnare, senza mai forzare, i gesti della quotidianità. Il minimalismo è solo apparente: in equilibrio con lavorazioni raffinate, accostamenti calibrati, tocchi decisi nei punti giusti. **Una sintesi precisa dello stile dello studio Sanayi313, che mette al centro il luogo, la persona e la misura**.

- [Un mini appartamento a Parigi di soli 35 m2, ispirato al minimalismo giapponese. Piccolo giardino incluso](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/piccolo-appartamento-parigi-epicene-minimalismo-giapponese-giardino/)
- [All’interno di un’antica corte salentina, questo trilocale di appena 35 metri quadri racconta la storia di un’amicizia diventata amore](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/casa-vacanze-puglia-piccola-coppia-ristrutturazione/)
- [17 case a Roma che ti faranno innamorare della loro bellezza, tra avanguardia e tradizione architettonica](https://www.ad-italia.it/article/case-roma-belle-idee-foto/)

title: Karl Lagerfeld : son ancien triplex et atelier de 300 m^2^ est à vendre près des quais de Seine
content:
## L'ancien triplex de Karl Lagerfeld est à vendre à Paris pour plusieurs millions d'euros
Depuis la disparition du regretté couturier et ancien directeur artistique de [Chanel](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/saint-tropez-boutique-chanel){: target="_blank"}, [Karl Lagerfeld](https://www.admagazine.fr/galerie/karl-lagerfeld-appartement-paris) en 2019, le grand public découvre son vaste patrimoine immobilier, entre ses villas à Monaco, ou encore son [pavillon à Louveciennes](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/karl-lagerfeld-maison-dormi-une-nuit) qui fut sa dernière maison.



[#image: /photos/685bd08b7d2f4458e65a4bf3]|||Le salon, également espace de réception de l'appartement-atelier du couturier Karl Lagerfeld.|||

Cette dernière propriété qu’il s’offrit dans la région parisienne était d'ailleurs [à nouveau sur le marché il y a quelques semaines](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/derniere-maison-karl-lagerfeld-vendre-encheres), elle aussi. Un professionnel de l’immobilier qui s’en était porté acquéreur au moment de la succession, entre 2022 et 2024, l'a confiée à la Chambre des notaires de Paris pour la vendre aux enchères. Cette demeure du XIXe siècle à la façade néoclassique, qui s’étend sur 600 mètres carrés était alors mise à prix pour 4 635 000 €. Mais aujourd’hui, c'est l'ancien intérieur parisien du Kaiser qui est mis en vente par l’agence immobilière De Ferla. Son ancien appartement et atelier de création, situé quai Voltaire, est annoncé au prix de 8 900 000 €. Visite de cet écrin unique en son genre.

### Un appartement-atelier atypique Rive Gauche, où règnent les arts décoratifs et le design


[#image: /photos/685bd0ecfcb9a1a886e87c40]|||Un large escalier dessert les chambres.|||

C’est un lieu aussi rare que précieux, où l’empreinte d’un génie de la [mode](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/jean-claude-jitrois-maison-vacances-ibiza) résonne encore dans chaque volume, chaque ligne, chaque matière. DeFerla Prestige dévoile en exclusivité un bien unique : l’ancien atelier de [Karl Lagerfeld](https://www.admagazine.fr/galerie/karl-lagerfeld-ancien-appartement-a-vendre), situé dans le très convoité VIIe arrondissement de Paris, à deux pas du quai Voltaire, dans un écrin de silence et de verdure, à l’abri des regards. Discrètement niché en fond de cour, ce lieu confidentiel de 318,85 m² au sol, dont 290 m² habitables, sur trois niveaux est bien plus qu’un simple appartement. Décrit comme un « *fragment d’histoire, un manifeste esthétique, un pan de la vie du Kaiser de la mode* » l’appartement est le témoin d’un mode de vie singulier, façonné par l’exigence, la curiosité et l’avant-garde.

### Un lieu aux lignes fluides réinventées, entre bois et surfaces métallisées


[#image: /photos/685bd0ac6d444450b9f1b2fd]|||La salle à manger aux lignes radicales.|||

C’est dans le quartier de Saint-Germain-des-Prés que Karl Lagerfeld recevait ses proches, esquissait ses visions créatives et dessinait ses collections iconiques. L’atelier, pensé par Karl Lagerfeld lui-même, porte encore la marque de sa vision créative: volumes sculpturaux, lignes futuristes, rampes en spirale, jeux de transparence et de matière... Le lieu est un hymne au design d’avant-garde. Revisité par la designer [Pauline Leprince](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/collectible-2024-les-pieces-de-design-a-retenir), sans trahir l’esprit des lieux, l’appartement dévoile des espaces tout en poésie. Bois précieux et acier brossé s’y côtoient avec naturel, dans une composition subtile où courbes douces et lignes radicales se répondent. Le mobilier, entièrement conçu sur mesure, s’intègre dans une scénographie pensée comme un tableau vivant.

### Un appartement avec son mobilier sur-mesure, comme le lit aux airs de vaisseau spatial


[#image: /photos/685bd0cc14f46646a490521c]|||Vue d'une des chambres avec ses volumes assumées.|||

La déambulation intérieure se veut à la fois fluide et spectaculaire. L’appartement compte deux [chambres](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/comment-amenager-chambre-amis) dont une suite magistrale, une immense [salle à manger](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/decorateurs-comprennent-pas-salles-manger-v2) ouverte sur une [cuisine](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/idees-organiser-cuisine-semi-ouverte-tendance) professionnelle, un bar convivial, une cave à vin sculpturale, ainsi qu’un espace bien-être hors normes avec salle de yoga, sauna et hammam. À la croisée entre résidence de luxe et du lieu de réception exclusif, cet ancien atelier dispose d’une rare autorisation à double usage : bureaux ou hébergement hôtelier. Il peut ainsi accueillir des événements professionnels de prestige, des séjours haut de gamme ou servir de pied-à-terre à un collectionneur, un investisseur ou un esthète. « *Le revenu locatif potentiel est estimé à 700 000 euros par an* », prévient l’agence De Ferla. Un bien unique où chaque pièce raconte une histoire, entre héritage et modernité.

L’appartement-atelier est à vendre par l’agence De Ferla pour 8 900 000 €.



[#image: /photos/685bd09214f46646a4905216]

+++pullquote

> *« Ce qui m’excite, c’est acheter \! »*, confiait-il.

Karl Lagerfeld

+++



[#image: /photos/685bd09a6d444450b9f1b2fb]



[#image: /photos/685bd0a214f46646a4905218]



[#image: /photos/685bd0bafcb9a1a886e87c3e]



[#image: /photos/685bd0d409c47adf69d8b321]



[#image: /photos/685bd0e37d2f4458e65a4bf5]

title: Gemmyo dévoile son nouveau flagship parisien, un écrin taillé sur mesure
content:
## Gemmyo dévoile son nouveau flagship parisien, un écrin taillé sur mesure
+++group-2

[#image: /photos/687616da6b5c1e3cee25db04]|||La devanture de la boutique, rue de Seine.|||

[#image: /photos/687616d0112d2e593436316b]|||La devanture de la boutique, rue de Seine.|||

+++

Inspiré des jardins du Luxembourg, la boutique parisienne nichée à Saint-Germain-des-Prés a été pensée comme une orangerie bucolique. Fondée en 2011 par Pauline Laigneau et son mari Charif Debs, la maison s’est imposée en quelques années comme l’étendard d’une joaillerie contemporaine, accessible et singulière. Derrière une façade aux lignes haussmanniennes, la boutique se transforme en [salon](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/guide-salon-assises-adopter) intime où les clients prennent le temps, échangent et essayent les pièces de joaillerie, dans une scénographie chaleureuse et feutrée. Découverte de l’écrin sur mesure.

### **Une adresse pensée comme un hôtel particulier à Saint-Germain-des-Prés**


[#image: /photos/687613fa823b84357852ec67]|||L'entrée aux allures de jardin parisien.|||

Après le [rénovation](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/espaces-abraxas-rehabilites-travaux-millions-euros) de la boutique rue Courcelles, Gemmyo dévoile un nouvel espace de 134 mètres carrés dans le quartier historique du VIe arrondissement de Paris. Pour l’occasion, la maison a de nouveau fait appel au duo d’architectes Stephan Bidoux et Julien Villeneuve pour repenser l’espace comme un lieu décontracté tout en faisant référence à l’art, la culture et les personnalités qui ont marqué l’histoire de ce lieu. « *De Juliette Gréco à [Serge Gainsbourg](https://www.admagazine.fr/article/maisons-jane-birkin-serge-gainsbourg), en passant par Simone de Beauvoir et Jean-Paul Sartre, la boutique se nourrit de l’héritage culturel du quartier* » rappelle la maison.

+++group-2

[#image: /photos/687614076b5c1e3cee25db00]

[#image: /photos/68761407de8bfefa7c5689fc]

+++

À l’intérieur, le raffinement discret du lieu dialogue avec une inspiration végétale assumée. Sol en terrazzo moucheté, arches arrondies, marbre, verre de Murano, teintes douces oscillant entre vert sauge, nude et ivoire… Chaque élément contribue à créer une atmosphère apaisante, presque résidentielle. Un mobilier sur mesure en bois blond et laiton brossé côtoie des pièces design dans une harmonie subtile entre tradition et modernité. Un clin d'œil élégant à l’esprit des orangeries du XVIIIe siècle, réinterprété ici dans une grammaire contemporaine.

### **Un lieu d’échange et de création**
+++group-2

[#image: /photos/6876140a16f4664f402b43c5]

[#image: /photos/687614016b5c1e3cee25dafe]

+++

Plus qu’un simple point de vente, le nouveau flagship parisien de Gemmyo se veut un lieu d’expérience. Dans l’arrière-boutique, une alcôve confidentielle accueille les rendez-vous personnalisés et les créations sur mesure. Un espace feutré, pensé comme un boudoir joaillier, où l’on prend le temps d’imaginer la pièce parfaite, alliance, bague de fiançailles ou bijou unique, accompagné par les experts de la maison. Une parenthèse précieuse, à l’image de la relation que Gemmyo entretient avec sa clientèle : personnalisée, chaleureuse et libre. Cette volonté de réenchanter l’expérience joaillière se retrouve dans chaque détail. À mi-chemin entre la galerie, le salon privé et l’atelier contemporain, la boutique brouille les frontières entre les univers. Des objets d’art curatés par Amélie Maison d’Art ponctuent le parcours, tandis que la lumière naturelle, filtrée par les ouvertures façon atelier, baigne l’ensemble d’une clarté douce et enveloppante.



[#image: /photos/6876141b9a4b6cfec6672d52]|||Boudoir privé pour les essayages de bijoux, avec aux murs les oeuvres de chez Amélie Maison d'Art.|||

En plein cœur de Saint-Germain-des-Prés, le 74 rue de Seine devient bien plus qu’une simple adresse : un lieu de vie, de transmission et de création, à l’image d’une maison joaillière qui réinvente avec finesse les codes du genre.

##### Boutique Gemmyo
##### 74 rue de Seine, 75006 Paris
##### [https://www.gemmyo.com](https://www.gemmyo.com/){: target="_blank"}


[#image: /photos/687613fcde8bfefa7c5689f6]



[#image: /photos/687614016b5c1e3cee25dafd]



[#image: /photos/68761403de8bfefa7c5689f9]



[#image: /photos/6876140d823b84357852ec69]



[#image: /photos/6876141216f4664f402b43c7]



[#image: /photos/68761444823b84357852ec6b]
